# The Jersey City Sound — Full Archive

> Every entry in the cited encyclopedia-archive of Jersey City ("Chilltown") music culture. Licensed CC BY-SA 4.0; free to quote with attribution.

## 14 Karat Soul
A cappella group — 1970s – 1990s

- 14 Karat Soul are an American a cappella and doo-wop revival group whose early recordings were issued through Jersey City's Catamount Records
- Working with Stan Krause's Catamount label, the group helped carry the vocal-harmony tradition into the 1980s, later touring internationally
- Launched by Stan Krause on Catamount Records, the group gained major popularity in Japan and reached national US audiences with a 1983 performance on Saturday Night Live
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-14-karat-soul.html

## 1stLady EL
Party promoter

- 1stLady EL is a Jersey City party promoter documented as a queen of the city's party scene
- Affiliated with the Murda Mamis, she is credited with over a decade of events, connections, and culture — from small rooms to sold-out nights — supporting female artists
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-1stlady-el.html

## Akon
Rapper

- Aliaune Damala Bouga Time Puru Nacka Lu Lu Lu Badara Akon Thiam (born April 16, 1973), known professionally as Akon ( AY-kawn), is a Senegalese-American singer, songwriter, record producer, businessman and philanthropist.
- Akon spent his high school years in Jersey City, attending Snyder High School and Dickinson High School, after the rest of his family moved to Atlanta.
- As a solo artist he reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 with “I Wanna Love You” (2006) and “Don't Matter” (2007).
- An influential figure in world music, he rose to prominence in 2004 following the release of his single "Locked Up" (featuring Styles P).
- Styled in hip-hop, it preceded the release of his debut studio album Trouble (2004), which became his commercial breakthrough and spawned the R&B-styled follow-up, "Lonely", the following year.
Sources: Wikipedia — Akon; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-akon.html

## Al Caiola
Musician

- Alexander Emil Caiola (September 7, 1920 -- November 9, 2016) was an American guitarist, composer, and arranger born in Jersey City, New Jersey, who became one of the most in-demand session guitarists of the twentieth century.
- He recorded over fifty albums and worked with Elvis Presley, Ray Conniff, Frank Sinatra, Percy Faith, Buddy Holly, Mitch Miller, Tony Bennett, and Astor Piazzolla, among many others.
- His work spanned jazz, country, rock, and pop, and his guitar playing is embedded in some of the most commercially successful recordings of the 1950s and 1960s.
Sources: Wikipedia — Al Caiola; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-al-caiola.html

## Al Di Meola
Musician

- Albert Laurence Di Meola (born July 22, 1954) is an American guitarist.
- Known for his work in jazz fusion and world music, he had his breakthrough after joining Chick Corea's Return to Forever group in 1974.
- He launched, from 1976 afterwards, a successful and critically acclaimed solo career, noted for his technical mastery, complex compositions and explorations of Latin music.
Sources: Wikipedia — Al Di Meola; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-al-di-meola.html

## Albee Al
Rapper

- Albee Al (born Albert Robinson, 1988) is a rapper and songwriter from Jersey City's Marion Projects, known for gritty, street-level storytelling; his catalog has drawn millions of streams across Apple Music, Spotify, and YouTube.
- He carries the stage name of his late father, Lord Sun Albee Al, founder of the pioneering Jersey City crew Sweet Slick and Slide — placing him in a direct line from one of Chilltown's foundational hip-hop families; DJ E Double and DJ Thunderkatt are his cousins.
- After being acquitted in 2014 of a 2010 murder charge — for which he had spent roughly four years in jail awaiting trial — he sharply increased his musical output, releasing projects including Everyday June 30th, a tribute to his murdered brother, and Koba (2019), which peaked at No. 6 on the Billboard Heatseekers Albums chart.
- He is affiliated with Flatline and Splashlyfe and is credited on DJ Swill B's 'Choppa in the Backseat' (2024); community voices count him among the city's current legends.
- He is a son of the late Lord Sun Albee Al, the Sweet Slick and Slide founder whose name he carries, and brother of DJ Thunderkatt and J-Sass, by family account given to this archive in 2026; DJ E Double is his cousin.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Archive cross-reference: DJ Swill B entry — 'Choppa in the Backseat' credit (2024); Family history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; NJ.com — “5 years after murder acquittal, Jersey City rapper Albee Al charged in shooting” (2019)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-albee-al.html

## Andrew Hill
Pianist

- Andrew Hill (June 30, 1931 – April 20, 2007) was an American jazz pianist and composer.
- Jazz critic John Fordham described Hill as a "uniquely gifted composer, pianist and educator" although "his status remained largely inside knowledge in the jazz world for most of his career."
Sources: Wikipedia — Andrew Hill; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-andrew-hill.html

## Anthony J. Cirone
Musician

- Anthony James Cirone (born November 8, 1941) is an American percussionist born in Jersey City, New Jersey, who served as principal percussionist of the San Francisco Symphony and was a Professor of Music at San Jose State University from 1965 to 2001.
- He is one of the most influential percussion educators of the late twentieth century, with textbooks and method books used in conservatories and university programs worldwide.
Sources: Wikipedia — Anthony J. Cirone; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-anthony-j-cirone.html

## Apache
Rapper

- Anthony Peaks (December 26, 1964 – January 22, 2010), better known as Apache, was an American rapper.
- Peaks emerged from New Jersey in the late 1980s as a front man for the Flavor Unit, a hip-hop group.
- He first appeared on the Flavor Unit album, The 45 King Presents The Flavor Unit, in 1990.
Sources: Wikipedia — Apache; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-apache.html

## Attrell Cordes
Rapper

- Attrell Cordes (May 19, 1970 – June 17, 2016), known as Prince Be, was the primary vocalist of Jersey City hip-hop duo PM Dawn and one of the most distinctive voices of the early 1990s alternative hip-hop era.
- He co-wrote and performed 'Set Adrift on Memory Bliss' (1991), which reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 and brought PM Dawn -- and Jersey City -- to the top of the American pop chart.
- His lyrical style blended introspection, spirituality, and surreal imagery in a way that set PM Dawn apart from every other act in early 1990s hip-hop.
Sources: Wikipedia — Attrell Cordes; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-attrell-cordes.html

## Basil Ruysdael
Musician

- Basil Ruysdael (1888 -- 1960) was an American actor, singer, and opera bass who was born in Jersey City, New Jersey, and performed on Broadway and in early Hollywood films through the 1940s and 1950s.
- He sang bass roles with major American opera companies before transitioning to acting, appearing in films including 'Come to the Stable' (1949) and 'Broken Arrow' (1950).
Sources: Wikipedia — Basil Ruysdael; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-basil-ruysdael.html

## Benito
DJ · Producer — c. 1995–2006+

- Benito is a Jersey City producer-DJ who was roughly 11 years into his career at the time of the 2006 WizTV documentary, placing his start around 1995.
- He is a practitioner of the 201 style of doubled drums -- the production signature that the documentary names as specific to Jersey City -- making him one of the clearest documented examples of that sound.
- He worked alongside Double O, and his interview is one of the most technically specific accounts of Jersey City's production culture in the entire film.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-benito.html

## Benny Blanca
Artist

- Benny Blanca is an artist embedded in the Jersey City music community, known for their contributions to the local scene.
- They were cataloged in the archive's survey, representing the dynamic and ever-evolving roster of city talent.
- Their music circulates through independent channels, connecting with the regional audience that supports homegrown arts.
- They stand as part of the modern wave of creators keeping the Jersey City Sound active and relevant.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-benny-blanca.html

## Beth Fowler
Musician

- Beth Fowler is an American actress and singer born in Jersey City, New Jersey, best known for her Broadway career and for playing Sister Ingalls in the Netflix series 'Orange Is the New Black'.
- She is a two-time Tony Award nominee for her Broadway work, as well as a two-time Screen Actors Guild Award winner for Outstanding Performance by an Ensemble in a Comedy Series as part of the 'Orange Is the New Black' cast.
- Her Broadway credits span several decades and include roles in major productions, establishing her as a versatile performer across musical theater and dramatic television.
Sources: Wikipedia — Beth Fowler; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-beth-fowler.html

## Big Spanish
Rapper

- Big Spanish is a Jersey City rapper known for his imposing presence and contributions to the local hip-hop scene.
- Documented in the archive's survey of active artists, he represents the strong intersection of Latino culture and hip-hop within the city.
- His style blends traditional street rap elements with personal narratives, delivered with authoritative lyricism.
- He remains a respected figure in the underground circuit, maintaining the city's reputation for authentic, hard-hitting rap.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-big-spanish.html

## Blaze (Blaze-In-Arts)
Designer — c. 2003–present

- Blaze, working under the brand Blaze-In-Arts, is the graphic designer behind the visual identity of Jersey City's mixtape era, called the scene's cover designer in the 2006 WizTV documentary.
- He has designed roughly 1,000 mixtape covers since 2003, each marked by his signature crown logo, making him one of the most prolific visual artists in the city's music history.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-blaze-in-arts.html

## Block Royal
Hip-hop group

- Block Royal is a prominent name within the Jersey City rap scene, recognized in the archive's survey of active hip-hop artists.
- Operating with a focus on street narratives and authentic representation, they contribute to the city's reputation for gritty, uncompromising lyricism.
- They utilize modern digital platforms and local networks to distribute their music, bypassing traditional industry gatekeepers.
- Their work reflects the resilience and independent spirit that characterizes the Hudson County hip-hop landscape.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-block-royal.html

## Bob Lido
Musician

- Robert Lido (September 21, 1914 -- August 9, 2000) was an American violinist and singer from Jersey City, New Jersey, who became a regular member of 'The Lawrence Welk Show', one of American television's longest-running musical programs.
- His appearances on the Welk Show brought him into millions of American living rooms across the program's run, making him one of the more widely seen musicians to come out of Jersey City.
Sources: Wikipedia — Bob Lido; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-bob-lido.html

## Bralikk Animalz
Hip-hop group

- Bralikk Animalz is a Jersey City rap collective known for their raw energy and deep ties to the local hip-hop community.
- They were cataloged during the archive's comprehensive survey of the city's active urban music scene.
- The collective structure allows them to showcase multiple vocal styles and lyrical perspectives, a hallmark of classic East Coast hip-hop crews.
- Their presence highlights the importance of group dynamics and shared platforms in the modern Jersey City underground.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-bralikk-animalz.html

## Brute Force
Musician

- Stephen Friedland (born September 29, 1940), known as Brute Force, is an American singer and songwriter from Jersey City, New Jersey, who was part of the New York avant-pop scene of the 1960s.
- He wrote and performed with The Tokens and wrote songs recorded by Peggy March, Del Shannon, The Chiffons, and The Cyrkle, building a reputation as an unconventional pop craftsman.
- His 1969 single 'King of Fuh' was released on Apple Records, making it one of the label's most unusual releases and earning him a lasting cult following.
Sources: Wikipedia — Brute Force; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-brute-force.html

## Catamount Records
Record label — 1964 – 2016

- Catamount Records is a Jersey City record label founded in 1964 by Stan Krause, proprietor of the Stan's Square Records store
- In its early years the label issued mostly doo-wop acappella records, later branching into soul, disco, and funk
- Krause and Catamount helped launch the careers of groups including The Persuasions, 14 Karat Soul, the Royal Counts, Vintage, and Jo Ann & the Heartaches
- Records were pressed in small runs — often as few as 25 and no more than 200 copies — including green, blue, red, and bronze colored vinyl now more sought-after than black pressings, made possible by Kay Records, the Little Ferry pressing plant owned by Krause's father
- Skip Jackson, formerly of the Shantons, played a key production role at the label until his death in 1982
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; Classic Urban Harmony — Stan Krause obituary (2016); Catamount discography — Discogs
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-catamount-records.html

## CF201 (Constant Flow)
Rapper · MC — 1993–present

- CF — Constant Flow, who records as CF201 — is a Jersey City rapper who has worked the underground hip-hop scene since 1993, coming up through freestyle battles in his hometown before joining the New York open-mic circuit at Washington Square Park cyphers and rooms like the Nuyorican Poets Café and End of the Weak.
- He has shared stages with Akon, Mobb Deep, and Immortal Technique, released his first official mixtape, Storm Mode, on Creative Juices Music in 2011, and has been counted among the Viper Records family.
- His 2026 album Pizza Warz is, in his words, “a love letter to Monmouth Street in Downtown Jersey City” and the old Italian neighborhood he grew up in — built around the real price war between two Monmouth Street pizzerias — produced by Soulful Soze, mixed by SpeedyBabyy, and carrying a tribute to the late rapper Brav Divi.
Sources: CF (Constant Flow) — official site (About); Constant Flow — Hip Hop from Jersey City, NJ — ReverbNation; “Chilltown rapper Constant Flow builds on actual Pizza Warz” — Chilltown Blues
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-cf201.html

## Champagne
MC · Manager · Filmmaker — c. 1980s – present

- Champagne, also known as Miss Champagne, is a Jersey City hip-hop pioneer — by the scene's account one of the city's first female MCs and artist managers — and the director and editor of the Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary.
- In the 1980s she was, for a time, a member of the Chiba (Cha) Crew, and the scene credits management of the conscious-rap group Positively Black to "Youth Champagne."
- Decades later she convened many of Chilltown's surviving pioneers on camera, producing the documentary that preserves the scene's first-person history.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-champagne.html

## Charlie Dixon
Musician

- Charles Edward Dixon (December 31, 1898, Jersey City, New Jersey -- December 6, 1940, New York City) was an American jazz banjoist, one of the earliest recorded jazz instrumentalists from Jersey City.
- Between 1921 and 1923 he was a member of Johnny Dunn's Original Jazz Hounds, recording for Columbia Records in New York -- among the earliest commercial jazz recordings produced in the country.
- He played in local ensembles before joining Sam Wooding's orchestra in 1922, a band that toured internationally and introduced jazz to European audiences.
Sources: Wikipedia — Charlie Dixon; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-charlie-dixon.html

## Charlotte Maconda
Musician

- Charlotte Maconda (March 12, 1863 -- May 15, 1952) was an American soprano singer from Jersey City, New Jersey, who performed and recorded in the early era of recorded sound -- the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.
- Her recordings, made during the acoustic era before electrical recording, preserve one of the earliest documented vocal performances by a Jersey City artist.
Sources: Wikipedia — Charlotte Maconda; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-charlotte-maconda.html

## Chiba Crew
DJ & MC collective — late 1970s – 1980s

- The Chiba Crew was a prominent Jersey City DJ and MC collective of the late 1970s and 1980s
- The crew helped shape the regional sound that bridged early disco, breakbeats, and the emerging hip-hop movement; DJ Thurmie Thurm was a founding member
Sources: Red Line Legacy — community history (Facebook)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chiba-crew.html

## Chill Divine
Rapper — c. 1990

- Chill Divine, born Gary Van Liew, is a 1990s underground hip-hop artist known for a 12-inch EP originally released on Square Records in 1990
- Square Records was founded by Stan Krause — the Jersey City record-store owner behind Stan's Square Records and Catamount Records — placing Chill Divine's debut within the Krause label lineage that runs from 1960s acappella and soul into the city's early hip-hop
- The record pairs “Puttin’ It Wildly” and “Long Live the Lyrical”; the remastered four-track release adds “Puttin’ It Wildly (Dub)” and “Long Live the Lyrical (Instrumental)”
- The music features boom-bap production and a classic lyrical style
- A great-grandson of Sheldon Van Liew through Kenneth and Ardelle Van Liew, and the uncle of DJ DX (Robert Van Liew), Chill Divine embedded the Van Liew name in Jersey City's foundational hip-hop — one of four cornerstone figures in the family's six-decade musical legacy
Sources: Discogs — Chill Divine, Puttin’ It Wildly / Long Live The Lyrical (12″, 1990, Square Records); “Puttin’ It Wildly” — 1990 Square Records (YouTube); Van Liew family history, contributed by Robert Van Liew (DJ DX), The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chill-divine.html

## Chill Rob G
Rapper

- Chill Rob G (born Robert Frazier) is a rapper from Jersey City, New Jersey, and a founding member of DJ Mark the 45 King's legendary Flavor Unit collective -- one of the defining hip-hop crews of the Golden Era.
- His 1989 debut album 'Ride the Rhythm' (Wild Pitch Records), produced by The 45 King, is widely cited as a classic of the era, featuring 'Court Is Now in Session' and 'Dope Rhymes'.
- His song 'Let the Words Flow' was sampled without permission by German dance group Snap! for their global hit 'The Power' (1990), one of the most commercially successful acts of unauthorized sampling in early 1990s pop.
- He returned with 'Empires Crumble' (2022, SpitSLAM/Chuck D's label), a boom bap comeback featuring a Flavor Unit reunion with The 45 King, Lakim Shabazz, and others, followed by 'Survival of the Better' (2026), executive produced by Chuck D and produced entirely by C-Doc.
- In the Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary he speaks at length about the "Let the Words Flow" / "The Power" saga — recounting how Snap!'s Turbo B performed the reworked material while his own version stood on the smaller Wild Pitch label — and recalls the early Chilltown DJs who would not let him grab the microphone before his rise.
Sources: Wikipedia — Chill Rob G; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chill-rob-g.html

## Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary
Documentary film — c. 2010s

- The Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary is a community-made film directed and edited by Champagne that gathers first-person interviews with the pioneers of Jersey City's hip-hop scene — the city long nicknamed "Chilltown."
- It traces the culture from its early-1980s park-jam and block-party roots through the crews, DJs, and MCs who built the scene, including Sweet Slick and Slide, the Tranquilizing Three, and Positively Black, with the Chiba Crew and others in the mix.
- On-camera voices include DJ Stranger Dee, DJ Wimpy Bee, Chill Rob G, DJ Nel E Nel, DJ 007, Prince Kyrie, and Cool Sir Brown, alongside visiting New York artists who frequented the city such as LA Sunshine of the Treacherous Three.
- The film doubles as an argument for the city's place in hip-hop history, repeatedly returning to why Chilltown — despite its 1980s prominence — never received the national recognition its pioneers feel it earned.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chill-town-jc-hip-hop-documentary.html

## Chop Diesel
Artist

- Chop Diesel is a Jersey City artist documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chop-diesel.html

## Chris Phoenix
Rapper

- Christopher Charles O'Neal, formerly known as MaLus the PoeT and better known by his stage name Chris Phoenix, is an American rapper and producer.
- Along with his partner Marcus Sibley, better known as Walkin Contradiction, he is the co-founder of the independent record label Beyond Musiq Records (BMR).
- He is the host of the Urban Juke Joint, a well-known poetry event held at the Baháʼí Center in NYC.
Sources: Wikipedia — Chris Phoenix; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chris-phoenix.html

## Christina Milian
Musician

- Christina Milian (born Christine Flores, September 26, 1981) is an American singer, songwriter, and actress from Jersey City, New Jersey, who debuted on the national stage with a feature on Ja Rule's 'Between Me and You' (2000), which peaked at No. 11 on the Billboard Hot 100.
- Her debut single 'AM to PM' (2001, Island Records) reached the top 40 of the Billboard Hot 100 and crossed over to UK charts, establishing her as an international pop presence.
- She has built a parallel acting career alongside her music, with television and film credits across network productions.
Sources: Wikipedia — Christina Milian; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-christina-milian.html

## Cito on the Beat
Producer

- Cito on the Beat is a Jersey City producer documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-cito-on-the-beat.html

## Claydes Charles Smith
Musician

- Claydes Charles Smith (September 6, 1948 -- June 20, 2006) was a Jersey City-born guitarist and founding member of Kool & the Gang, the funk, soul, and R&B group that grew from Jersey City's Lincoln High School scene in 1964.
- As the band's lead guitarist for more than four decades, he contributed to the distinctive riff-driven, horn-section sound that made Kool & the Gang one of the most sampled and commercially successful groups in American popular music.
Sources: Wikipedia — Claydes Charles Smith; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-claydes-charles-smith.html

## Cool Sir Brown
Rapper

- Cool Sir Brown is an independent hip-hop artist from Jersey City, New Jersey, known for classic and modern rap tracks across a catalog available on Spotify, Apple Music, Audiomack, Amazon Music, and Deezer.
- His single and album 'Sir Brown Lives' is the centerpiece of his catalog, a project rooted in vintage Jersey City hip-hop tradition.
- He has collaborated with the late hip-hop legend Biz Markie and maintains an active release schedule into 2025.
- Born Seth Brown, he is documented in the Chill Town film competing in a landmark MC battle at P.S. 11, backed by a doo-wop harmony section arranged by his sister, the songwriter Mary Brown — an early Jersey City showcase for the family's talent.
Sources: Cool Sir Brown — Spotify; Cool Sir Brown — Apple Music; Cool Sir Brown — Audiomack; Van Liew family history, contributed by Robert Van Liew (DJ DX), The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-cool-sir-brown.html

## Craiga Ski
MC · Producer · Promoter — c. early 1990s – present

- Craiga Ski, also billed Craig Ski, was a lifelong Jersey City native, an emcee and promoter of the city's early 1990s hip hop scene, and a core member of the underground group Maniac Mobb.
- He moves in the circles of the Flavor Unit, the Jersey born collective around Queen Latifah, and in Maniac Mobb stood alongside Double J, the Flavor Unit founding member.
- In 1992 he appeared with Jersey City artist Double J on the very first broadcast of a Jersey City cable television music program, a moment the program's own tribute recalls.
- In later years he worked in the local film scene as an associate producer and writer on projects made in and around Jersey City, by community account.
- He produced most of the tracks on Double J's 1991 album The Hitman (4th and Broadway), the beats done in house by the two of them, including If It Ain't A Caddy, It Ain't A Car.
- Double J told the story of how they met: Craigaski left a cassette of beats on his car with an invitation to come check him out; he answered the door in Bootsy Collins glasses with Christmas lights blinking in the basement and the beats banging, and since he lived around the corner the two clicked and worked from then on.
Sources: Unkut: The Hitman Double J (production credit and the meeting story, told by Double J); Jersey City cable program tribute post (Facebook): the program's first 1992 broadcast featured Double J and Craiga Ski; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-craiga-ski.html

## Cy Coben
Musician

- Seymour 'Cy' Coben (April 4, 1919 -- May 26, 2006) was an American songwriter from Jersey City, New Jersey, whose hits were recorded by bandleaders, country singers, and popular artists across the mid-twentieth century.
- His songs reached unlikely audiences: The Beatles recorded his 'Some Other Guy', Tommy Cooper recorded his comedy material, and Leonard Nimoy recorded his compositions, reflecting the range of his output.
- His country songwriting in particular produced hits recorded by artists on major Nashville labels through the 1950s and 1960s.
Sources: Wikipedia — Cy Coben; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-cy-coben.html

## Cyclone Static
Musical group

- Cyclone Static is an American punk rock band from New Jersey that has actively performed in and around the Jersey City independent music circuit.
- Their sound channels the aggressive, melodic energy of classic 1970s and 1980s punk, updated for modern underground audiences.
- They have shared stages with notable punk acts and released music that resonates with the regional DIY and punk communities.
- The band represents the enduring appeal of raw, guitar-driven punk rock within New Jersey's vibrant alternative landscape.
Sources: Wikipedia — Cyclone Static; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-cyclone-static.html

## D-Eastwood
Rapper

- D-Eastwood is a Jersey City hip-hop artist on the Many Men Records roster.
- This entry has been opened and awaits fuller biographical detail and receipts from the community.
Sources: Many Men Records roster and label history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-d-eastwood.html

## DamnGirll
Rapper

- DamnGirll is a distinct voice in the Jersey City music community, recognized for their contributions to the local arts scene.
- Documented during a survey of active artists, they represent the creative diversity flourishing within the city.
- Their artistic output adds to the broad spectrum of genres and styles that define contemporary Hudson County.
- They maintain an independent presence, connecting with audiences through local networks and digital platforms.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-damngirll.html

## David Kikoski
Musician

- Dave Kikoski (born September 29, 1961) is an American jazz pianist and keyboardist from New Jersey who has been one of the most in-demand sidemen in the New York jazz scene for more than three decades.
- He has recorded and performed with Roy Haynes, Woody Shaw, Eddie Henderson, and many others, and has released a strong catalog as a leader that demonstrates his command of bebop and post-bop vocabulary.
Sources: Wikipedia — David Kikoski; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-david-kikoski.html

## De$igner Boyz
Hip-hop group

- De$igner Boyz are a Jersey City hip-hop group with roots across Curries Woods, Sal-Laf Courts, Bidwell & Ocean, and Lexington Avenue
- Their catalog, produced largely by Uncle YG and Herogawd, includes “Mama Rich,” “Fine$$er,” and “Do or Die 2 & 3”
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-designer-boyz.html

## Deejay Luchionney
DJ · Radio founder — 1995 – present

- Deejay Luchionney, also known as DJ Luchionney, is a veteran Jersey City DJ and the C.E.O. of Hood Work Radio
- DJing since 1995, he spins across all types of events and is an active figure in the city's contemporary DJ community
Sources: Oral history: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (Hood Work Radio; DJing since 1995)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-deejay-luchionney.html

## Dennis "Dee Tee" Thomas
Musician

- Dennis 'Dee Tee' Thomas (February 9, 1951 -- August 7, 2021) was a founding member of Kool & the Gang, one of the group's most distinctive voices and instrumentalists, known for his alto saxophone playing, flute, and his role as the group's on-stage announcer.
- He grew up in Jersey City, New Jersey, attending Lincoln High School alongside the other founding members, and was with the band from its formation in 1964 through its final years, one of the most consistent presences in a decades-long lineup.
Sources: Wikipedia — Dennis "Dee Tee" Thomas; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dennis-dee-tee-thomas.html

## Diana Soviero
Musician

- Diana Soviero (born March 19, 1942, in Jersey City) is an American operatic soprano of international stature, a recipient of the Richard Tucker Award in 1979.
- Soviero studied at the Juilliard School of Music with Florence Berggren, Marinka Gurewich, Martin Rich, and Boris Goldovsky.
- She made her debut under the name Diana Catani-Soviero at the Chautauqua Opera in 1969 as Mimi in La Boheme.
Sources: Wikipedia — Diana Soviero; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-diana-soviero.html

## Dibiasi
Rapper

- Dibiasi is an artist operating out of Jersey City, New Jersey, noted in the archive's catalog of the active music scene.
- He brings his unique stylistic approach to the city's collaborative and competitive creative environment.
- Like many independent artists in the region, he relies on grassroots promotion and community support to advance his craft.
- His inclusion in the roster ensures that the working artists shaping the current cultural moment are properly recorded.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dibiasi.html

## Dickie Thompson
Musician

- Dickie Thompson was an American musician with strong Jersey City connections who navigated both the jazz and popular music landscapes of his era.
- His versatility allowed him to perform across different contexts, adapting to the shifting musical tastes of the mid-twentieth century.
- Artists like Thompson represent the working-class musicianship that defined Jersey City's contribution to the broader New York metropolitan music industry.
- His career highlights the interconnected nature of jazz, R&B, and pop ensembles that frequently drew on Hudson County talent.
Sources: Wikipedia — Dickie Thompson; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dickie-thompson.html

## DidThatt
Rapper

- DidThatt is a musical artist from Jersey City, New Jersey, recognized in the archive's comprehensive survey of local talent.
- Their moniker suggests a bold, confident approach to their craft, a common trait among the city's competitive artists.
- They operate within the independent scene, contributing to the ongoing narrative of Hudson County music.
- Their inclusion preserves their footprint in the contemporary era of the city's cultural history.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-didthatt.html

## Dino Danelli
Musician

- Dino Danelli (born Robert Daniel, July 23, 1944 -- December 15, 2022) was an American drummer and an original member of the Young Rascals, the rock group whose sound defined a moment of 1960s blue-eyed soul.
- He has been called 'one of the great unappreciated rock drummers in history', a reputation built on his technical command and his ability to hold the Rascals' hybrid of soul, gospel, and rock together.
Sources: Wikipedia — Dino Danelli; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dino-danelli.html

## Dirt Bike Annie
Musical group

- Dirt Bike Annie was a prominent pop punk and power pop band formed in New York City with deep ties to the New Jersey and Jersey City scenes.
- Active from the late 1990s through the mid-2000s, they were known for their highly melodic, hook-driven songwriting reminiscent of the classic Lookout Records catalog.
- They released multiple albums and EPs, building a dedicated cult following through relentless touring and energetic live shows.
- Their cross-river presence highlights the fluid exchange of musicians and bands between New York's downtown scene and New Jersey's underground venues during that era.
Sources: Wikipedia — Dirt Bike Annie; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dirt-bike-annie.html

## DJ 007
DJ — c. 1991 – present

- DJ 007, born Michael Williams and also known as Styles 007 and Mr. Shut Em Down, and formerly as DJ Worm, is a Jersey City DJ who has operated under all of these aliases across a career of more than three decades, first documented in the 2006 WizTV documentary.
- Although he spoke in the 2006 film of plans to retire in 2007, he never stepped away — DJ 007 remains an active Jersey City DJ.
- He sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records alongside the city's top DJs, placing him in the commercial heart of Jersey City's mixtape distribution network.
- He is named among the community's favorite Jersey City DJs in the Chill Town documentary, cited alongside DJ Nel E Nel and DJ Mad Money.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @007_style201 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-007.html

## DJ All Out
DJ — c. 2000s

- DJ All Out is a Jersey City DJ documented on flyers from the early-2000s club circuit, billed on the Summer Heatwave 2002 card alongside E-Square and Chameleon (Ka-Million) among "Jersey City's Finest."
- This entry has been opened from the flyer record and awaits fuller biographical detail and receipts from the community.
Sources: Event flyers from the DJ Q (Da Topic) archive, 2000–2003 — The Jersey City Sound; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-all-out.html

## DJ Bam
DJ

- DJ Bam is a Jersey City DJ who has built his presence within the city's extensive network of local performers and event curators.
- His chosen handle, @djbam201, firmly aligns him with the Jersey City and Hudson County area, a region known for its fierce loyalty to homegrown talent.
- He was identified and cataloged during the archive's effort to document the contemporary DJ community that keeps the city's venues and parties energized.
- His work represents the ongoing continuum of Jersey City DJ culture, bridging the gap between historical mixtape eras and modern digital promotion.
Sources: Instagram @djbam201 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-bam.html

## DJ BigTime
DJ · Producer — c. 1990s–2006+

- DJ BigTime is a Jersey City mixtape DJ active from the 1990s through at least 2006, known in the scene for street-focused mixtape music -- a lane he describes on camera in the 2006 documentary, showing his home studio, crates, and full setup.
- He originally performed as DJ Quality before taking the name Big Time, a rebranding that marked his transition into a more prominent role in Jersey City's mixtape distribution network.
- Mark Cee credited DJ BigTime with getting Mark Cee's final mixtape (around 2000 to 2001) distributed, reflecting the informal but real influence he had over how music moved through the city.
- He served as a mentor figure to DJ Dolo, who says he came up looking up to Big Time -- the kind of generational mentorship that defines how DJ skill has always moved through Jersey City.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-bigtime.html

## DJ Count Basil
DJ · Producer — c. 1980s – present

- DJ Count Basil, also known as Brutha Basil, is a house music DJ and producer originally from Jersey City, deeply connected to the New York and New Jersey club scenes, with past residencies at iconic venues including the Paradise Garage, Nell’s, and the Sound Factory.
- As a producer he is known for classic tracks such as “City Streets” and “Hardhaus II” on the influential Nu Groove label, and he is celebrated for his deep, classic, and soulful house sets.
- He is also remembered in the Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary as the DJ Basil who first put DJ Wimpy Bee on at a Lincoln High School party — an early node in the city’s DJ lineage.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (Nu Groove discography; Paradise Garage / Nell’s / Sound Factory residencies); Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-count-basil.html

## DJ Dolo
DJ — c. 2000s

- DJ Dolo is a Jersey City DJ of the Dangerous Minds crew who performed earlier as Dollar Mike before settling on the Dolo name, a transition documented in the 2006 WizTV documentary.
- He credits DJ BigTime as a mentor who shaped his approach to the craft, reflecting the mentorship networks that passed DJ skills across generations in the Jersey City scene.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-dolo.html

## DJ DYCE
DJ

- DJ Dyce is a Jersey City DJ who is likely the figure shouted out as 'Dice' in the 2006 WizTV documentary, one of several names called out by peers during the film's interview segments.
- He sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records alongside the city's top DJs and is active on Instagram as @djdyce201, where the 201 area code confirms his Jersey City identity.
- Period flyers document him across the Jersey City battle-and-club circuit — spinning music alongside DJ Q at Winter Blaze 2000, appearing on Laydown-N-Die Records' “Dream Team” poster with DJ 007, DJ Nel E Nel, and DJ E Double, and billed among the invited DJs at Rap Wars Round 2 in 2003.
Sources: Instagram @djdyce201 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Event flyers from the DJ Q (Da Topic) archive, 2000–2003 — The Jersey City Sound
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-dyce.html

## DJ E Double
Legend DJ · Promoter · Producer — 1995 – present

- DJ E Double, born Eric Pinkney and also known as DJ E-Dub DaGeneral and E Platinum, is a Jersey City DJ celebrated as the Prince of Jersey -- one of the city's most prominent mixtape and club DJs of the late 1990s and early 2000s.
- In 1995 he founded his entertainment brand DpEnt. (Double Platinum Entertainment), which became the foundation for his promotions, party hosting, and mixtape distribution across the East Coast.
- Through the early 2000s he built a reputation for signature blends, club mixes, and custom tracks defining North Jersey's urban nightlife, with residencies at Rendezvous (formerly Ruthie's), Lollipop, Taste, Brenda's, and Bills.
- He is interviewed in the 2006 WizTV documentary, where he gives a shoutout to the Killa K family, documenting the social fabric of the scene.
- His daughter performs as DJ She Dog, making him part of a family lineage in the city's DJ tradition.
- He was the founder and head of Double Platinum Entertainment. The crew's members included Greenbean, Thunderkatt, Smurfet, DJ Q, DJ DYCE, Deejay Luchionney, DJ Walleykatt, DJ DX (Double X), and Exclusive (R.I.P Jamie).
- He is a nephew of Lord Sun Albee Al, founder of the pioneering crew Sweet Slick and Slide, and a cousin of the rapper Albee Al and DJ Thunderkatt — tying him to one of Chilltown's foundational hip-hop families.
- His Double Platinum mixtape series ran deep into the catalog, with surviving tapes including volumes 21, 33, 36, and 41; volume 41 paired him with Jersey City's Joe Budden as E-Platinum Double on Desert Storm Records.
- His collaborations on tape include The Red Tape with DJ Q for Dream Team Entertainment and Double Threat with DJ 007, released with a party at the Boys and Girls Club.
- In 2002 he hosted the street album HEAT (Tell Em We Coming) featuring Murda, Big Panic, and Crisis, promoted with a Jersey Journal styled front page announcing it in stores June 1, 2002.
- He stands in the Exit 14 DJs crew photographs preserved in this archive, alongside DJ Walley Katt, So Swift, DJ J-Money, DJ SupermanSpin, DJ Flash, DJ T-Fresh, DJ Yadabee, and DJ Freeze.
Sources: DJ E-Dub DaGeneral — SoundCloud; WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @djedubdageneral — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Oral history: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (Eric Pinkney; “Prince of Jersey”; founding of DpEnt., 1995); Family history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; DJ E Double personal archive: tapes, photographs, and promo artifacts, shared with The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (see gallery)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-e-double.html

## DJ Flamez
DJ

- DJ Flamez is a Jersey City DJ working the North Jersey party and Jersey club circuit.
- His ReverbNation profile lists him as a DJ based in Jersey City, New Jersey; he posts mixes online as dj-flamez-201 on SoundCloud — the “201” marking Jersey City's area code — and as @therealdjflamez on Audiomack.
- On New Year's Eve 2017 he shared the bill with fellow Jersey City DJ Rydeout at Beer House in Bloomfield, New Jersey, one of the North Jersey rooms the two have worked.
Sources: DJ Flamez — ReverbNation; DJ Flamez (@therealdjflamez) — Audiomack; New Year's Eve 2017, Beer House Bloomfield — Bloomfield Center
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-flamez.html

## DJ Flash
DJ · Mixtape pioneer — c. 1970s–present

- DJ Flash, born Ronald Hamilton, is a Jersey City DJ from Park Street who is widely credited in the 2006 WizTV documentary as the originator of the city's mixtape scene and was introduced on camera as a legend.
- He began DJing at age seven, learning by convincing older neighborhood DJs including Stranger D to let him spin -- a start that placed him at the founding generation of Jersey City hip-hop.
- He practiced all five elements of DJing including beat juggling, scratching, and mixing, and describes pre-sampler production built with two turntables and two tape decks, a workflow typical of the late 1970s and early 1980s park jam era.
- He was part of a five-DJ juggle-battle circle from his block, crediting peers including Jamaican Jeff and E Rock as early inspirations, and names Wimpy B as his big brother in the craft.
- He also credits a generation that includes Stranger D, Fresh Jeff, and Storming Norman (later moving with Wu-Tang's circle), placing the Jersey City scene in direct connection with one of hip-hop's defining crews.
- He operates a store in Jersey City referenced on camera, mentions 271 Ocean Avenue, and stepped back from full-time DJing to raise his children while remaining active in the scene.
- He also records as DJ Flash Hamilton and works within the New Jersey Mix Kings 201 collective alongside DJ Hazz B and Semaj da DJ.
- By his own account, given to this archive in 2026, he co-produced Double XX Posse's debut single "Executive Class" (Mel-O Records, 1990), handling the drums in a session that also drew on DJ Wimpy Bee, DJ Stranger Dee, and the brothers Sugar Chris and Magic.
- By his own account, given to this archive in 2026, the 1950s Jersey City R&B star Roy Hamilton was his great-great-grandfather, and the late Jersey City rapper Riggamortis was his first cousin; he records as DJ Flash Hamilton.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-flash-jersey-city.html

## DJ Freeze
DJ

- DJ Freeze is a Jersey City DJ and a member of the Exit 14 DJs collective.
- He stands with the crew in the E14DJS photographs preserved in this archive, identified by the community, 2026.
Sources: Exit 14 DJs crew photographs, community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-freeze.html

## DJ Handz
DJ · Turntablist

- DJ Handz is a Jersey City turntablist, one of the working DJs of the city's contemporary scene.
- His ReverbNation profile identifies him as a DJ and turntablism artist based in Jersey City, New Jersey, and he posts mixes to SoundCloud.
Sources: DJ Handz — ReverbNation; DJ Handz — SoundCloud
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-handz.html

## DJ Hazz B
DJ

- DJ Hazz B (also styled DJ Hazz-B) is a Jersey City hip-hop and mixtape DJ active in the region's mixtape culture, known for his mashups, custom remixes, and seamless a cappella blends of classic hip-hop and rap.
- He performs and collaborates under the collective New Jersey Mix Kings 201, working alongside fellow New Jersey turntablists including DJ Nyce Riley, DJ Junebugg, DJ Flash Hamilton, and Semaj da DJ.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (surfaced via search; pending verification)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-hazz-b.html

## DJ Infamous
DJ

- DJ Infamous is a Jersey City DJ who operates as an integral part of the local hip-hop and club landscape, proudly representing the city's signature 201 area code.
- His handle @inf_201 deliberately centers his local identity, firmly distinguishing him from the Atlanta-based DJ Infamous who works in an entirely different commercial market.
- Like many stalwarts of the Jersey City scene, his reputation was forged through local performances and community ties rather than mainstream commercial releases.
- His inclusion in the archive preserves the footprint of the working DJs who provide the soundtrack to Jersey City's nightlife and neighborhood events.
Sources: Instagram @inf_201 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-infamous-jersey-city.html

## DJ J Dub
DJ

- DJ J Dub is a Jersey City DJ embedded in the city's rich history of mixtape creators and party rockers.
- He is widely believed to be the figure whose name was garbled as 'J Dun' in DJ E Double's segment of the seminal 2006 WizTV documentary, an error clarified by recent community identification.
- This correction restores his proper place in the historical record of the late-1990s and 2000s era, when a shoutout on a documentary or mixtape carried significant weight.
- He maintains his connection to his roots through his @jdub2o1 handle, honoring the Jersey City area code that defined his era.
Sources: Instagram @jdub2o1 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-j-dub.html

## DJ J Nice
DJ

- DJ J Nice (@officialjnice1) is a DJ working across the New Jersey and New York club and event circuit, and part of the wider Jersey City DJ network.
- He is the official DJ for Jordan Brand's Jumpman “The One,” for Dyckman Basketball, and for the Shake the Block series.
- He is a separate person from DJ Nyce (@djnyceriley) — a distinct area DJ whose similar name is frequently confused with his.
Sources: DJ J Nice on Instagram (@officialjnice1); Identity clarification (DJ J Nice is not DJ Nyce), DJ DX (Robert Van Liew), The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-j-nice.html

## DJ J-Money
DJ · Producer — 2021–present

- DJ J-Money is a Jersey club DJ and producer working in the Jersey City scene.
- His recording catalog runs from early-2020s singles — “Bando Boss Royalty Intro” (2021), “Grip That Back,” “Point Em Out,” and “Green Light” featuring Money Choc (2022) — through “DJ J-Money Beat Goes On” (2025) and “Time to Play the Game” (2026), alongside featured spots on Jersey club releases.
Sources: DJ J-Money — Apple Music
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-j-money.html

## DJ Joe Smith
DJ

- DJ Joe Smith is a Jersey City DJ of the founding generation.
- He stands in the Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait by Tijon Maro, preserved in this archive, among the seven pioneers on the stoop.
Sources: Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait, Tijon Maro (see DJ Flash's gallery); Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-joe-smith.html

## DJ Junebugg
DJ

- DJ Junebugg is a New Jersey hip-hop and mixtape DJ and a member of the New Jersey Mix Kings 201 collective, alongside DJ Hazz B and Semaj da DJ.
- This entry has been opened and awaits fuller biographical detail and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (surfaced via search; pending verification)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-junebugg.html

## DJ K (Killa K)
DJ

- DJ K, also known as Killa K, is a Jersey City DJ whose 'Killa K family' received a direct shoutout from DJ E Double (Double Platinum Entertainment) in the WizTV Jersey City DJ Documentary (2006).
- This prominent mention places him firmly in the city's late-1990s and 2000s mixtape scene, a highly competitive era where crew affiliations and shoutouts were markers of street credibility.
- His identity and affiliation were confirmed by DJ DX (Robert Van Liew) in an oral history recorded for The Jersey City Sound in 2026, anchoring his place in the scene's historical record.
- Operating under the Instagram handle @coalitionboy_k, the 'Coalition' in his name signifies his continued affiliation with the tight-knit networks that define Jersey City's DJ culture.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Oral history: DJ DX (Robert Van Liew), 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-k.html

## DJ Mad Money
DJ — c. 1989–present

- DJ Mad Money — born James Gadsden, and billed over the years as Mad Money and Man Money — is a Jersey City mixtape DJ active since approximately 1989, one of the longest-running figures in the scene's documented history.
- He is the producer of the Street Blends series, including Phase 20 and Phase 21 — tape series spanning decades that reflect the sustained output of a career DJ.
- In the 2006 WizTV documentary he recalls the era when a Jersey City mixtape could earn a DJ $2,000 or more, and names Devil Seven as a peer from that period, placing the city's tape economy in historical context.
- His classic, high-energy “Jersey City-style” mixes from the Chilltown circuit remain highly regarded, and he is counted among the influential DJs of the city's hip-hop and mixtape community.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Community identification (real name; one person across billings, confirmed by DJ DX, 2026)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-mad-money.html

## DJ Madden
DJ · Producer · Recording artist — c. 2000s–present

- DJ Madden, born Steve Garcia, is a Jersey City DJ, turntablist, and producer who was put on by DJ Wizard and mentored by Mark Cee, placing him in the direct lineage of the city's most influential scene-builders.
- He collaborated with DJ DX on a trilogy of studio albums: 'This Is Hip-Hop', 'The Unfortunate Child', and 'Made From Scratch' (2015, Apple Music), marking a formal recording output that sets him apart from many mixtape-only peers.
- Beyond their album trilogy, he and DJ DX have released a run of underground hip-hop and rap singles — including “1000 Reasons” and “Keeps Me Going” — pairing classic boom-bap with the reggaeton and Latin-urban energy the pair are known for working into their live sets.
- He sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records alongside the city's top DJs and is active on Instagram as @officialdjmadden.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @officialdjmadden — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Made From Scratch — DJ DX & DJ Madden, Apple Music; DJ DX artist profile, about.me/djdx (albums with DJ Madden); “1000 Reasons” and “Keeps Me Going” — DJ DX & DJ Madden (Apple Music; Spotify); Mixtape cover art from the DJ Madden archive (Yahway Music) — The Jersey City Sound
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-madden.html

## DJ NEL E NEL
DJ · Producer

- DJ NEL E NEL, born Nelson Carty and also known as Mandela and billed as Nel E Nel Blends, is a Jersey City blends mixtape legend, DJ, and producer with credits extending from the local scene to nationally known artists.
- His production and blends credits include work with Joe Budden, Ransom, PnB Rock, and N.O.R.E., and per the archive's founding oral history, his resume also includes credits with Kodak Black and the duo Hitchcock and Ransom.
- He is said by the scene's oral tradition to have put out a Notorious B.I.G. album ahead of its official release, a claim that awaits a second source but reflects his standing as a first-tier figure in Jersey City's mixtape culture.
- By community account he won the Winter Blaze 2000 DJ battle held at the Jersey City Boys and Girls Club, the city's premier battle competition of that era.
- He sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records alongside the city's top DJs and remains active on Bandcamp and Instagram as @nelenelblends.
- He is repeatedly named among the community's favorite Jersey City DJs in the Chill Town documentary, cited across generations alongside DJ Wimpy Bee.
- He is part of the Presidential Beats production team — the beat-making arm of Orlando Wharton's Presidential — alongside Producer Mayor.
Sources: DJ Nel E Nel — Bandcamp (blends catalog and production credits); DJ NEL E NEL — blends mix (YouTube); DJ NEL E NEL — blends mix (YouTube); DJ Nel E Nel — Facebook; Instagram @nelenelblends; Oral history: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (Winter Blaze 2000; additional credits); Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-nel-e-nel.html

## DJ Nyce
DJ

- DJ Nyce (@djnyceriley), also billed as DjNyce Riley, is a mixtape and club DJ in the New Jersey 201 scene.
- He records the Alchemy Mix Kings mixtape series alongside area DJs including DJ Hazz B and DJ June Bugg.
- He is a separate person from DJ J Nice (@officialjnice1), with whom his name is frequently confused.
Sources: DJ Nyce on Instagram (@djnyceriley); Identity clarification (DJ Nyce is not DJ J Nice), DJ DX (Robert Van Liew), The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-nyce.html

## DJ P Dub
DJ — c. late 1990s–2000s

- DJ P Dub is a Jersey City mixtape DJ who was active in the late 1990s and 2000s, named in the 2006 documentary during DJ DX's account of the era alongside Showtime and DJ Q.
- He collaborated with DJ DX on several mixtape series, with each tape running more than 70 tracks -- a volume reflecting the scene's competitive, high-output mixtape culture of that period, as described in DJ DX's 2026 oral history.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Oral history: DJ DX (Robert Van Liew), founder interview for The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-p-dub.html

## DJ Q
DJ — c. late 1990s – 2000s

- DJ Q — billed on records and flyers as DJ "Q" and known today as Da Topic — is a Jersey City mixtape and party DJ who came up through the city's late-1990s and 2000s club, mixtape, and battle circuit.
- He was part of the Stan's Square Records mixtape generation, sharing bills and battle cards with peers including DJ 007, DJ Nel E Nel, DJ Dyce, DJ E Double, and DJ P Dub.
- Flyers from the period place him across the era's marquee events — battling live at the 1st Annual Winter Blaze mixtape DJ competition in 2000, appearing on Laydown-N-Die Records' "Dream Team" poster, and billed at Summer Heatwave 2002 among "Jersey City's Finest."
- He held club nights across the city and neighboring Newark — from Hipnotiq Thursdays at Foxes on St. Paul's Avenue to Stand Up Fridays at the Paradise Club on Broadway — and was among the invited DJs for Rap Wars Round 2 in 2003.
- He is named in both DJ DX's and DJ 007's segments of the 2006 Jersey City DJ Documentary; his identity was confirmed by DJ DX in 2026.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @da_topic_81 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Event flyers from the DJ Q (Da Topic) archive, 2000–2003 (Winter Blaze 2000; Laydown-N-Die Records "Dream Team"; Summer Heatwave 2002; Rap Wars Round 2, 2003; Hipnotiq Thursdays; Stand Up Fridays; Summer Maddness 2002) — The Jersey City Sound
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-q.html

## DJ Rydeout
DJ

- DJ Rydeout is a Jersey City DJ active on the North Jersey club and event circuit.
- His ReverbNation profile lists him as a DJ based in Jersey City, New Jersey; he also keeps an audience on Instagram as @djrydeout and posts mixes to SoundCloud.
- He has shared bills with fellow Jersey City DJ Flamez, including a New Year's Eve 2017 date at Beer House in Bloomfield, New Jersey.
Sources: Dj RydeOut — ReverbNation; DJ Rydeout (@djrydeout) — Instagram; New Year's Eve 2017, Beer House Bloomfield — Bloomfield Center
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-rydeout.html

## DJ Stranger Dee
DJ · Pioneer — early 1980s –

- DJ Stranger Dee is a legendary figure in the Jersey City hip-hop and DJ scene who gained local fame in the early 1980s as a foundational DJ and pioneer for the Sweet, Slick and Sly Crew -- one of the most celebrated acts in early New Jersey hip-hop.
- Along with his crew, Stranger Dee performed at seminal events including New Jersey vs. Bronx showdowns at the Benmore Skating Rink in Jersey City; key documented dates include a January 15, 1982 showdown with the Cold Crush Brothers and the Turnout Battle of 82 on July 2 of that year, both preserved in flyers archived at the Museum of Pop Culture (MoPOP).
- He is credited by DJ Flash -- who names Stranger D as an influence who let him spin as a child -- with being a founding presence in the park-jam scene that built Jersey City's DJ tradition from the ground up.
- He made contributions on a national scale, working as a DJ on major hip-hop tours alongside groups including Public Enemy in the late 1980s, taking the Jersey City sound to a national touring circuit.
- He is regarded by older generations and hip-hop historians as a founding father of the Chilltown (Jersey City) music movement.
- Multiple artists in the Chill Town documentary name him the first DJ they ever heard in Jersey City — director Champagne recalls hearing him cutting break beats in Armstrong Park — underscoring his role as a founding presence in the city's park-jam scene.
- DJ Flash, in a firsthand account given to this archive in 2026, credits him with supplying the instrument sample on Double XX Posse's debut single "Executive Class" (Mel-O Records, 1990).
Sources: MoPOP Museum — Sweet, Slick and Sly Crew, Jersey City; MoPOP Museum — Benmore presents The Turnout: Battle of '82; Hoboken Girl — Jersey City's “Chilltown” history; Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-stranger-dee.html

## DJ SupermanSpin
DJ

- DJ SupermanSpin — Joel Grizzly — is a Jersey City DJ and a co-owner of the local DJ collective Exit 14 DJs.
- By his own social-media profiles he DJs for Exit 14 DJs and serves as station manager for the crew's Exit 14 DJs app, and gives Jersey City, New Jersey as home.
Sources: DJ SupermanSpin (@djsupermanspin) — Instagram; SupermanSpin (Joel Grizzly) — Facebook; SupermanSpin — SoundCloud
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-supermanspin.html

## DJ Swill B
DJ · Producer — 2020–present

- DJ Swill B is a Jersey City DJ and producer active since 2020, known for Jersey club production and collaborations across New Jersey rap.
- He is a featured artist on Bandmanrill's 2022 single 'Jiggy in Jersey' (feat. Sha EK), from the album Club Godfather, one of the defining Jersey club releases of that year.
- His catalog includes 'My Name' (feat. Mr. Chicken, 2022), 'Roll'n' (2023), 'Down 4 Mine' (2024), and 'COMMITMENT' (2025), available on Apple Music and major platforms.
- He appeared in Mr. Chicken's 'Never Could' video (2021), covered by No Jumper, introducing the Jersey City scene to a national audience.
- Additional credits include 'Shake Dat' with Asian Doll (2022) and 'Choppa in the Backseat' with Albee Al (2024).
Sources: Apple Music artist page; No Jumper: Mr. Chicken 'Never Could' feature; Shazam releases
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-swill-b.html

## DJ T-Fresh
DJ

- DJ T-Fresh is a Jersey City DJ and a member of the Exit 14 DJs collective.
- He stands with the crew in the E14DJS photographs preserved in this archive, identified by the community, 2026.
Sources: Exit 14 DJs crew photographs, community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-t-fresh.html

## DJ Thunderkatt
DJ — c. 2000s

- DJ Thunderkatt is a Jersey City DJ pictured with DJ Q, DJ P Dub, and DJ E Double in photographs from the DJ Q archive.
- This entry has been opened from the photographic record and awaits fuller biographical detail and receipts from the community.
- He is a son of the late Lord Sun Albee Al, founder of Sweet Slick and Slide, and brother of the rapper Albee Al and of J-Sass, by family account given to this archive in 2026; DJ E Double is his cousin.
Sources: Event flyers from the DJ Q (Da Topic) archive, 2000–2003 — The Jersey City Sound; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Family history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-thunderkatt.html

## DJ Thurmie Thurm
Battle DJ · Pioneer — late 1970s – 1980s

- DJ Thurmie Thurm, also known as DJ Thurm, is a battle DJ and pioneer of the original Jersey City, New Jersey hip-hop and house scene, best known for his work in the late 1970s and early 1980s as a founding member of the Jersey City Chiba Crew.
- He came up in the Chilltown scene -- Jersey City's nickname -- playing park jams, community centers, and local spots alongside legends including Grand Wizard Capone, Nicky Barnes, and the Sweet, Slick and Sly Crew.
- He is featured in the documentary 'Chill Town J.C.', directed by A. Champagne Lloyd, which documents the hip-hop artists who developed their talents in the streets of Jersey City and places him alongside Chill Rob G, Lakim Shabazz, Master Cee, and DJ Wimpy Bee as a city pioneer.
- Decades on, he remains a respected figure in Chilltown history, celebrated across East Coast music history circles and documented in community groups and online archives dedicated to Jersey City's founding era.
Sources: Instagram @originaldjthurm; Red Line Legacy — community history (Facebook)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-thurmie-thurm.html

## DJ Walley Katt
DJ — c. 2000s–present

- DJ Walley Katt, referenced in the 2006 WizTV documentary as Wiley Cat, is a Jersey City DJ and the partner of X5, forming one of the documentary's on-camera partnerships.
- He sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records alongside X5 and remains active on Instagram as @djwalleykatt as of 2026.
- He is a son of Peter Barnes, founder of the legendary Jersey City hip-hop group Double X Posse; he gave DJ DX his name, drawn from the group.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @djwalleykatt — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-walley-katt.html

## DJ Wimpy Bee
DJ · Blends pioneer

- DJ Wimpy Bee (Edmund Bryant) was a Jersey City DJ legend celebrated as a founding figure of the Chilltown hip-hop scene and one of the most respected DJs in the city's history, known for technical mastery including super-fast scratches and deep command of the turntables.
- He was named by DJ Flash as his 'big brother' in the craft -- the most direct form of mentorship credit in the scene -- placing Wimpy Bee at the top of the generational chain that built Jersey City's DJ tradition.
- He was featured in documentaries chronicling the city's unsung hip-hop pioneers, including the Chill Town Jersey City documentary, and his influence spanned decades from early block parties and school events through club sets and recorded mixtapes.
- Edmund Bryant passed away in October 2025. His death was met with an outpouring of respect from the Jersey City community, where he was remembered not only as a master of the turntables but as a 'special gentleman' deeply embedded in the city's cultural fabric.
- In the Chill Town documentary, DJ after DJ names him their favorite; he recounts spinning nearly every venue in the city — his staple at P.S. 11 School, plus St. Patrick's, Sacred Heart, Foxes and the city's churches — and taking up the craft after a cousin, DJ Slick, told him he could not do it, starting out on borrowed Panasonics before moving to Numark turntables.
- DJ Flash, in a firsthand account given to this archive in 2026, names him among the producers behind Double XX Posse's debut single "Executive Class" (Mel-O Records, 1990).
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006) — DJ Flash segment; Oral history: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (founder of the Jersey City blends tradition); Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-wimpy-bee.html

## DJ Yadabee
DJ

- DJ Yadabee, born Keneyada Thompson, is a Jersey City blends DJ whose handle @djyadabee_blends identifies his specialty: the blend-driven mixtape style that defines the city's 201 DJ tradition.
- He has little surviving web footprint beyond his Instagram, which is exactly the kind of underdocumented figure the Jersey City Sound archive was built to record before the history disappears.
Sources: Instagram @djyadabee_blends — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-yadabee.html

## Don Ciccone
Musician

- Donald Joseph Ciccone (February 28, 1946 – October 8, 2016) was an American singer, songwriter and musician.
- He was a founding member of the pop group the Critters, singing their biggest hits "Younger Girl" and "Mr.
- Dieingly Sad".
Sources: Wikipedia — Don Ciccone; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-don-ciccone.html

## Double J
Rapper — late 1980s – 1990s

- Double J (Gerald 'J' Jones), known as The Hitman Double J, is a Jersey City rapper and a founding member of Queen Latifah's original Flavor Unit collective, one of the defining hip-hop crews of the late 1980s and early 1990s.
- Growing up in Jersey City he hosted block parties alongside future stars including Apache; in 1988 he joined DJ Mark the 45 King's Flavor Unit after an introduction through Apache and the crew.
- He appears on the 1990 posse cut 'Flavor Unit Assassination Squad' (4th and Broadway/Island Records) and released the 12-inch 'Cannibal Town / Def Style' and solo album 'The Hitman', best known for the track 'Double J'z Tantrum'.
- He later resurfaced as half of the Maniac Mob, reuniting with DJ Mark the 45 King for production on several independent releases including a track on the 1995 compilation 'The D&D Project'.
- He received his Island Records deal after Fab Five Freddy saw a homemade video of the Flavor Unit crew and passed it up the chain -- a chain of events that shows how the early 1990s rap industry actually worked.
- His passing is mourned in community tribute as the loss of one of Chilltown's finest emcees and a Flavor Unit pioneer; the Jersey City cable program whose first 1992 broadcast featured him alongside Craiga Ski counted that moment among its proudest.
- He pressed Cannibal Town himself in 1988 on Chanpen Records, a label named for the aunt who put up the money: 3,000 copies, with the instrumental Club Trax on the flip because the 45 King knew club records moved through Abbey at Movin' Records in East Orange.
- His Jersey City school ties ran through the Flavor Unit itself: he and Latee sat in the same third grade class and he came up with Apache, and by his account it was Lakim Shabazz who named the Flavor Unit, the Flavor taken from Latee's This Cut's Got Flavor.
- The Hitman's beats were done in house by Double J and Craigaski, and the Gangster Hit video ran on Jersey City's two dollar call in cable video channel; when Island was absorbed by Polygram the album's push dissolved, and he walked away to business and family with, in his words, his own peace of mind.
Sources: Unkut — The Hitman Double J; Red Bull Music Academy — Flavor Unit oral history; The 45 King Presents the Flavor Unit — Apple Music
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-double-j.html

## Double XX Posse
Rap group — c. 1990 – 1995

- Double XX Posse were a Jersey City rap group of the early 1990s. The trio was billed as Sugar Ray, Peter Barnes, and DJ BK.
- Their debut single, "Executive Class" backed with "On A Mission," came out in 1990 on Jersey City's Mel-O Records, with label production credits to Mel-O Odoms and Ray Howell; Big Beat reissued it in 1991 and signed the group.
- By the firsthand account of DJ Flash, a co-producer on the session (given to this archive in 2026), "Executive Class" was built by a room of Jersey City DJs: Flash on the drums, DJ Stranger Dee supplying the instrument sample, brothers Sugar Chris and Magic creating the bassline, and DJ Wimpy Bee also producing.
- Their 1992 single "Not Gonna Be Able to Do It" reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot Rap Singles chart, and the follow-up "Head Cracker" peaked at No. 15; the hit also appeared in a season 3 episode of The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air.
- They released the album Put Ya Boots On in 1992 on Big Beat, an Atlantic subsidiary, and Ruff, Rugged and Raw in 1995 on Atlantic, which carried the Lord Finesse produced single "Money Talks."
- The group's name traveled with the city's own: the 1995 Usenet Rap Dictionary defined "Chilltown" as "Jersey City, Double XX Posse," and Jersey City DJ and archive founder DJ DX took his name from the group.
Sources: Double X Posse, Wikipedia; Executive Class / On A Mission (Mel-O, 1990), Discogs; Dig Of The Day: Double XX Posse, Executive Class (1990), Blackout Hip Hop; The Totally Unofficial Rap-Dictionary, rec.music.hip-hop (Usenet), July 14, 1995; Oral history: DJ Flash, co-producer of Executive Class, as told to The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-double-xx-posse.html

## E-Square
DJ — c. 1990s – 2000s

- E-Square is a Jersey City DJ of the mixtape and club era, billed on period flyers among "201 Squad's & Jersey City's Finest."
- He shared bills with DJ Q and other DJs of the Stan's Square Records generation, appearing at events such as Summer Heatwave 2002 at Club Millenium and Stand Up Fridays at the Paradise Club.
Sources: Event flyers from the DJ Q (Da Topic) archive, 2000–2003 — The Jersey City Sound; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-e-square.html

## Ed Shaughnessy
Musician

- Ed Shaughnessy (January 29, 1929 -- February 24, 2013) was an American jazz drummer from Jersey City, New Jersey, best known as the house drummer for 'The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson' from 1962 to 1992, a thirty-year run that made him one of the most heard drummers in American television history.
- Before The Tonight Show, he recorded and performed with leading jazz figures including Dizzy Gillespie, Coleman Hawkins, and Benny Goodman, establishing his credentials in the bebop and swing traditions.
Sources: Wikipedia — Ed Shaughnessy; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-ed-shaughnessy.html

## Exit 14 DJs
DJ collective

- Exit 14 DJs are a Jersey City DJ collective — their name taken from Exit 14, the New Jersey Turnpike exit for Jersey City — working under the slogan “We All One.”
- The crew keeps an Instagram presence as @exit_14djs and has built a branded Exit 14 DJs app; DJ SupermanSpin (Joel Grizzly) is among its co-owners.
- Crew photographs preserved in this archive show the brotherhood in matching E14DJS shirts at a Jersey City park event: DJ E Double, DJ Walley Katt, So Swift, DJ J-Money, DJ SupermanSpin, DJ Flash, DJ T-Fresh, DJ Yadabee, and DJ Freeze among them.
Sources: Exit 14 DJs (@exit_14djs) — Instagram; DJ SupermanSpin (@djsupermanspin) — Instagram (Exit 14 DJs)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-exit-14-djs.html

## Fit for an Autopsy
Musical group

- Fit for an Autopsy is an American deathcore band from Jersey City, New Jersey, formed in 2008.
- The band consists of guitarists Will Putney, Pat Sheridan and Tim Howley, drummer Josean Orta, lead vocalist Joe Badolato, and bassist Peter "Blue" Spinazola.
- They are currently signed to Nuclear Blast and have released seven studio albums since formation.
Sources: Wikipedia — Fit for an Autopsy; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-fit-for-an-autopsy.html

## Flip Wilson
Comedian · Entertainer — 1960s to 1970s

- Flip Wilson, born Clerow Wilson Jr. in Jersey City on December 8, 1933, grew up in the city and became one of the most popular American comedians of the early 1970s.
- His comedy album The Devil Made Me Buy This Dress won the 1971 Grammy Award for Best Comedy Recording.
- His comedy albums charted on the Billboard 200, an unusual crossover for a spoken word act. (The exact peak position is pending source confirmation before it is printed.)
- He hosted The Flip Wilson Show on NBC from 1970 to 1974, one of the highest rated programs of its era.
Sources: Wikipedia — Flip Wilson; Grammy Awards database
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-flip-wilson.html

## Florence Turner-Maley
Musician

- Florence Turner-Maley (August 23, 1871 – January 3, 1962) was an American composer, singer, and teacher.
- Florence Turner was born in Jersey City, New Jersey, to William Hayward and Mathilde (Holwill) Turner.
- Her father had been a boy soprano.
Sources: Wikipedia — Florence Turner-Maley; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-florence-turner-maley.html

## Frank Infante
Guitarist · Musician — 1970s to present

- Frank Infante was born in Jersey City on November 15, 1951, and became the guitarist of Blondie through the band's commercial peak.
- As a member of Blondie he played on the albums Parallel Lines, Eat to the Beat, and Autoamerican.
- Those records carried four Billboard Hot 100 number 1 singles, credited to the band: “Heart of Glass” (1979), “Call Me” (1980), “The Tide Is High” (1980), and “Rapture” (1981).
- He was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame as a member of Blondie in 2006.
Sources: Wikipedia — Frank Infante; Rock and Roll Hall of Fame — Blondie (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-frank-infante.html

## Frank Sinatra Jr.
Musician

- Frank Sinatra Jr. (January 10, 1944 -- March 16, 2016) was an American singer and musician, the son of Frank Sinatra, who spent much of his career as his father's musical director and conductor.
- He was kidnapped in December 1963 and held for ransom for several days before being released unharmed, an event that made national headlines and briefly overshadowed his early career.
- As a performer and conductor he carried the Great American Songbook tradition forward, touring extensively as both a solo artist and his father's musical director from the 1980s until his death.
Sources: Wikipedia — Frank Sinatra Jr.; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-frank-sinatra-jr.html

## George Brown
Musician

- George 'Funky' Brown (January 15, 1949 -- November 17, 2023) was an American drummer born in Jersey City, New Jersey, and a founding member of Kool & the Gang.
- He co-founded the group in the mid-1960s alongside Robert 'Kool' Bell and other Jersey City neighbors, all of whom attended Lincoln High School, and served as the rhythmic anchor of the band for more than five decades.
- As the group's primary drummer, he helped craft a sound blending jazz roots with hard funk and later crossover R&B, producing a catalog that is among the most sampled in music history.
Sources: Wikipedia — George Brown; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-george-brown.html
