## George Washington Gregory
Musician

- George Washington Gregory (February 15, 1867 – May 2, 1902) was a banjo player, playing in the classic banjo style.
- A composer, he arranged classical music for the 5-string banjo.
- He taught the banjo as a music teacher and wrote a musical method, Practical Fingering for the Banjo, originally published in serial.
Sources: Wikipedia — George Washington Gregory; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-george-washington-gregory.html

## Gerry Rosenthal
Musician

- Gerry Rosenthal is an American musician, singer-songwriter and actor.
- He has starred in films and short films and has guest starred in television series such as Law & Order and Celebrity Deathmatch.
- He is best known for providing the voice for Jimmy Hopkins, the main playable character in the 2006 video game Bully, for which he was nominated for a 2006 Spike VGA Award for Best Performance by a Human Male and a 10th Annual Interactive Achievement Award for Outstanding Achievement in Character Performance - Male.
Sources: Wikipedia — Gerry Rosenthal; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-gerry-rosenthal.html

## Get Focused Entertainment
Nightlife & events company · Party promoter

- Get Focused Entertainment (GFE) is a Jersey City-area nightlife and events company — a party-promotion and event-planning brand that works the North Jersey and New York club circuit.
- The company runs recurring party series including Jersey Drip, whose tenth-anniversary edition ran across July 18–19, 2026, alongside its Yacht Life boat parties on the Hudson and a weekly Karaoke & R&B Therapy night.
- It operates on Instagram as @gfefocus, where it has built an audience of more than ten thousand followers, and gathers its links through linktr.ee/gfefocus.
Sources: Get Focused Entertainment LLC (@gfefocus) — Instagram; Get Focused Entertainment — Linktree
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-get-focused-entertainment.html

## Gil Melle
Musician

- Gil Melle (December 31, 1931 -- December 28, 2004) was an American jazz musician, painter, and electronic music composer from Jersey City, New Jersey, who was among the first jazz artists to record as a leader for Blue Note Records.
- His early Blue Note sessions in the 1950s featured a hard bop and progressive jazz approach; he later moved into electronic music and became a pioneering composer of science fiction film and television scores, including the original 'The Andromeda Strain' (1971) soundtrack.
Sources: Wikipedia — Gil Melle; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-gil-melle.html

## Grand Wizard Capone
DJ — late 1970s – present

- Grand Wizard Capone is a Jersey City DJ of the park jam generation, named by DJ Thurmie Thurm as one of the legends he came up playing alongside.
- A period flyer for The Showdown For All DJs at the Benmore Skating Rink bills Capone alongside Strang D, Jimmy M, Al Bee, Sir Brown, and the era's crews.
- He stands in the Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait by Tijon Maro, preserved in this archive, and in the Duncan Projects reunion photographs with DJ Flash, both in Duncan Projects Jersey City shirts.
Sources: The Showdown For All DJs flyer, Benmore Skating Rink, preserved in this archive (see Lord Sun Albee Al's gallery); Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait, Tijon Maro (see DJ Flash's gallery); Named in DJ Thurmie Thurm's documented history
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-grand-wizard-capone.html

## Grandmaster Crash
DJ

- Grandmaster Crash is a Jersey City DJ of the founding generation.
- He stands in the Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait by Tijon Maro, preserved in this archive, among the seven pioneers on the stoop.
Sources: Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait, Tijon Maro (see DJ Flash's gallery); Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-grandmaster-crash.html

## Hao Huang
Musician

- Hao Huang is an accomplished American pianist, scholar, and educator with significant ties to Jersey City, New Jersey.
- He is known for his virtuosic concert performances spanning classical repertoire and contemporary compositions.
- Beyond his performance career, Huang is respected for his extensive research and writing on music, culture, and the intersection of arts and society.
- His dual roles as an active performer and an academic make him a uniquely influential figure in the contemporary classical music landscape.
Sources: Wikipedia — Hao Huang; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-hao-huang.html

## Harold Adamson
Musician

- Harold Campbell Adamson (December 10, 1906 -- August 17, 1980) was an American lyricist born in Greenville, New Jersey, who became one of the most prolific popular songwriters of the mid-twentieth century.
- He collaborated with composers including Jimmy McHugh, Burton Lane, and Hoagy Carmichael, writing lyrics for films, Broadway productions, and recordings across more than three decades.
- His songs include 'Around the World', 'Everything I Have Is Yours', and 'Time on My Hands', featured in Hollywood productions from the 1930s through the 1960s.
Sources: Wikipedia — Harold Adamson; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-harold-adamson.html

## Heather B. Gardner
Musician

- Heather B. Gardner (born November 13, 1970), known professionally as Heather B., is a rapper from Jersey City who first gained national visibility as a cast member of 'The Real World: New York' (MTV, 1992), the inaugural season of reality television's most influential franchise.
- Before her television debut she had already been affiliated with Boogie Down Productions, KRS-One's influential hip-hop group and one of the defining acts of socially conscious rap.
- She later returned to hip-hop as a recording artist and has worked as a radio personality, building a career spanning music, television, and broadcasting.
Sources: Wikipedia — Heather B. Gardner; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-heather-b-gardner.html

## Hitchcock
Rapper — c. late 1990s–present

- Hitchcock is a Jersey City rapper, one half of the A-Team alongside Ransom, whose partnership placed them among the sharpest punchline duos on the New Jersey mixtape circuit of the early 2000s.
- The A-Team were affiliates of DJ Clue's Desert Storm and released the Hardhood Classics mixtape trilogy before splitting around 2006.
- In the A-Team era the duo was managed by Orlando Wharton — later a Capitol Music Group EVP and president of Priority Records — as part of his Presidential Crew.
Sources: AllMusic — Ransom biography (A-Team); Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Industry and community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-hitchcock.html

## Howard Wyeth
Musician

- Howard Pyle Wyeth (April 22, 1944 – March 27, 1996), also known as Howie Wyeth, was an American drummer and pianist.
- Wyeth is remembered for work with the saxophonist James Moody, the rockabilly singer Robert Gordon, the electric guitarist Link Wray, the rhythm and blues singer Don Covay, and the folk singer Christine Lavin.
- Best known as a drummer for Bob Dylan, he was a member of the Wyeth family of American artists.
Sources: Wikipedia — Howard Wyeth; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-howard-wyeth.html

## Illanoise
Artist

- Illanoise is a Jersey City artist documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-illanoise.html

## J-Sass
Rapper

- J-Sass is a Jersey City musical artist who was identified and cataloged as part of the city's active creative community.
- Her work is a component of the broad cultural output that defines contemporary Hudson County.
- She participates in the local arts ecosystem, utilizing modern tools to distribute her music independently.
- She represents the enduring drive of local artists who continue to build the Jersey City Sound from the ground up.
- She is a daughter of the late Lord Sun Albee Al and sister of DJ Thunderkatt and the rapper Albee Al, by family account given to this archive in 2026; DJ E Double is her cousin.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-j-sass.html

## J. Fresh
Rapper

- J. Fresh is a Jersey City hip-hop artist on the Many Men Records roster.
- This entry has been opened and awaits fuller biographical detail and receipts from the community.
Sources: Many Men Records roster and label history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-j-fresh.html

## Jack Frost
Musician

- John Dempsey, known professionally as Jack Frost, is an American guitarist.
- He is one of the founders of the heavy metal band Seven Witches and also a part of The Bronx Casket Company.
- Frost is also known for playing guitar on Savatage's tour in support of Poets and Madmen in 2001 and 2002 before being dismissed from the band for unspecified reasons.
Sources: Wikipedia — Jack Frost; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jack-frost.html

## Jade
Vocalist

- Jade is a musical artist operating within the Jersey City, New Jersey, arts community.
- She was formally documented in the archive's roster of active local talent, ensuring her place in the city's cultural record.
- Her artistic endeavors contribute to the rich, multi-genre tapestry that characterizes modern Hudson County.
- She continues to develop her sound and reach audiences through independent and local networks.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jade.html

## Jane Harvey
Musician

- Jane Harvey (born Phyllis Taff, January 6, 1925 – August 15, 2013) was an American jazz singer, known for recording several tracks with famous musicians such as Duke Ellington and Benny Goodman during the 1940s.
- Phyllis Taft was born on January 6, 1925, in Jersey City, New Jersey.
- She auditioned for Barney Josephson, who offered her an opportunity to perform at his Greenwich Village's nightclub, Café Society.
Sources: Wikipedia — Jane Harvey; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jane-harvey.html

## Jerry Herman
Musician

- Gerald Sheldon Herman (July 10, 1931 -- December 26, 2019) was an American composer and lyricist born in Jersey City, New Jersey, whose Broadway musicals made him one of the most commercially successful theater songwriters of the twentieth century.
- His shows include Hello, Dolly! (1964), which ran for 2,844 performances and at one time was the longest-running Broadway musical in history; Mame (1966), a vehicle for Angela Lansbury; and La Cage aux Folles (1983), the first hit Broadway musical about a gay couple.
- The title song from Hello, Dolly! became a hit for Louis Armstrong (1964) and reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100, extending Herman's reach from the theater to popular radio.
Sources: Wikipedia — Jerry Herman; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jerry-herman.html

## Jim Lord
Singer

- James Edward Lord III (born September 7, 1948) is an American folk and rock singer-songwriter and musician with ties to Jersey City and Hudson County, New Jersey.
- He has released recordings in the folk-rock tradition and has been part of the regional New Jersey and New York singer-songwriter community.
Sources: Wikipedia — Jim Lord; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jim-lord.html

## Jimmy Lyons
Saxophonist

- Jimmy Lyons (December 1, 1931 – May 19, 1986) was an American alto saxophone player.
- He is best known for his long tenure in the Cecil Taylor Unit.
- Lyons was the only constant member of the band from the mid-1960s until his death.
Sources: Wikipedia — Jimmy Lyons; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jimmy-lyons.html

## Joe Bananas
DJ — c. 2000s

- Joe Bananas is a Jersey City DJ of Bananas Entertainment and Chemical Records who is interviewed in the 2006 WizTV documentary and noted for working in the chopped and screwed style -- a Southern technique he adapted to the Jersey City mixtape format.
- His segment in the documentary is dedicated in memory of Jay Vito (RIP), making it one of the film's most personal moments and reflecting the losses the scene had already absorbed by 2006.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-joe-bananas.html

## Joe Budden
Rapper · Podcast host — c. 2001–present

- Joe Budden is a rapper and media personality raised in Jersey City from age 13, who moved from the city's mixtape circuit to a national profile and is one of the most commercially successful artists the city has produced.
- His single 'Pump It Up' (2003) reached No. 38 on the Billboard Hot 100 and earned a Grammy nomination for Best Male Rap Solo Performance.
- He is a founding member of the rap supergroup Slaughterhouse (with Royce da 5'9, Joell Ortiz, and Crooked I), signed to Eminem's Shady Records. Slaughterhouse's album Welcome to: Our House debuted at No. 2 on the Billboard 200 in 2012.
- He has hosted The Joe Budden Podcast since 2015, surpassing 900 episodes and becoming one of hip-hop's most successful independent podcast platforms.
- In the Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary, older pioneers repeatedly invoke him as the city's most nationally visible rapper while debating why Chilltown's foundational scene never received wider recognition.
Sources: Wikipedia — Joe Budden; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-joe-budden.html

## John P. Hammond
Musician

- John P. Hammond (born November 13, 1942) is an American blues singer, guitarist, and harmonica player from New York who has recorded for Vanguard and other labels and is recognized as one of the most dedicated white blues revivalists of his generation.
- He is the son of legendary Columbia Records producer John Hammond Sr. and has performed with figures including Mike Bloomfield, Dr. John, and Duane Allman over a career spanning more than six decades.
Sources: Wikipedia — John P. Hammond; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-john-p-hammond.html

## Johnny Rotella
Musician

- Johnny Rotella (November 4, 1920 -- September 11, 2014) was an American woodwind player, session musician, and songwriter from Jersey City, New Jersey, whose career spanned more than six decades.
- He wrote over 200 songs, including the Frank Sinatra standard 'Nothing but the Best', and his woodwind playing appears on countless recordings from the 1940s through the 1990s as one of the most in-demand session reed players in New York.
Sources: Wikipedia — Johnny Rotella; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-johnny-rotella.html

## Joseph Trapanese
Musician

- Joseph Trapanese is an American composer, arranger, and producer from Jersey City, New Jersey, who has worked extensively in film, television, and video game scoring.
- His credits include composing music for 'Oblivion' (2013), 'The Greatest Showman' (2017), 'Tron: Uprising' (animated series), and 'Straight Outta Compton' (2015), establishing him as one of the more successful film composers with Jersey City roots.
Sources: Wikipedia — Joseph Trapanese; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-joseph-trapanese.html

## Juggs
DJ

- Juggs, formerly known under the alias DJ E Murda, is a Jersey City DJ who has navigated multiple eras of the city's local scene.
- His rebranding from his earlier moniker represents a common evolution among working DJs adapting their image as their careers and the local culture mature.
- He was identified by community historians and peers, emphasizing the tight-knit, word-of-mouth nature of Jersey City's DJ networks.
- He remains active and connected to the current scene through his @juggs201 handle, continuing to represent the city's foundational 201 area code.
Sources: Instagram @juggs201 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-juggs.html

## Kamillion
Producer · DJ — c. 2000s–present

- Kamillion — formerly billed as Chameleon and Ka-Million, and earlier as DJ Solid — is a Jersey City producer and DJ.
- He operates Paid In Full Studios and Ful Real Entertainment, a company named in memory of a friend known as Ful — a tribute that reflects the loyalty at the center of his work.
- He is credited as a producer across the city's scene and remains active on Instagram as @mixedbykamillion.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @mixedbykamillion — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-chameleon-ka-million.html

## Kate Micucci
Musician

- Kate Micucci ( mih-KOO-chee; born March 31, 1980) is an American actress, comedian, musician, and street artist who is half of the musical comedy duo Garfunkel and Oates with Riki Lindhome.
- Her acting roles include Stephanie Gooch in Scrubs, Ally in 'Til Death, Shelley in Raising Hope, Lucy in The Big Bang Theory, Sadie Miller in Steven Universe, Velma Dinkley in Scooby-Doo, Webby Vanderquack in DuckTales, Daisy in Nature Cat, Julie in Motorcity, Stacey in Guillermo del Toro's Cabinet of Curiosities, and Dr. Fox in Unikitty!
Sources: Wikipedia — Kate Micucci; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-kate-micucci.html

## Kenny Kenn
Videographer · Platform builder

- Kenny Kenn was a Jersey City videographer and platform builder who gave the city's unsigned artists a stage long before social media existed to fill that role.
- He created DJ Kenny Kenn's Unsigned Hype DVD, which documented and promoted unsigned Jersey City talent during the DVD era -- a format that preceded YouTube as the primary way local artists reached audiences.
- He produced the Promise Me Tomorrow documentary series and is remembered by peers as a staple of Jersey City culture who 'gave us all a shot to be on the tube way before social media.'
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community tributes comments (2026); confirmed by archive, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-kenny-kenn.html

## Kid Buu
Rapper

- Kid Buu is a rapper from Jersey City, New Jersey, who built his following on the underground rap circuit and has released music on streaming platforms including Spotify and Apple Music.
- He is known for an aggressive street rap style and has collaborated with other artists from the New Jersey and New York independent rap scenes.
Sources: Wikipedia — Kid Buu; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-kid-buu.html

## Killer Force
Hip-hop crew — c. 1980s

- Killer Force were a Jersey City hip hop crew of the park jam and skating rink era.
- A period flyer for The Showdown For All DJs at the Benmore Skating Rink carries their name across the bill from Sweet S and Sly, and the It's Magic breakin contest flyer lists them among the invited crews.
Sources: The Showdown For All DJs and It's Magic contest flyers, preserved in this archive (see Lord Sun Albee Al's gallery); Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-killer-force.html

## Kit McClure
Musician

- Kit McClure (born 1951) is a jazz musician, bandleader of the all-female big band Kit McClure Band and founder of the Women in Jazz Project.
- In 2004, she started a project to revive interest in the all-female big band, International Sweethearts of Rhythm.
- She has been a featured player with the Barry White Orchestra and toured with Sam & Dave.
Sources: Wikipedia — Kit McClure; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-kit-mcclure.html

## KNOXX
Artist

- KNOXX is a Jersey City artist documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-knoxx.html

## Kool & the Gang
Musical group

- Kool & the Gang formed in Jersey City, New Jersey, in 1964, founded by brothers Robert 'Kool' Bell and Ronald Bell along with five neighborhood friends who all attended Lincoln High School in the city.
- Before settling on their name the group went through earlier identities including the Jazziacs, the Soul Town Band, the New Dimensions, and Kool & the Flames.
- They honed their sound in Jersey City basements, at St. John's Church, and at the Boys and Girls Club on Ash Street before achieving national and international success across funk, soul, R&B, and pop.
- Their catalog is among the most sampled in music history, and Jersey City honored them in 2016 by officially renaming a section of Maple Street 'Kool & the Gang Way'.
- Between 1969 and 1987 the group built one of the most enduring chart histories in R&B and pop, with 25 Top-10 R&B hits, 9 Top-10 pop hits, and 31 gold and platinum albums; worldwide album sales exceed 70 million.
- Their 1970s street-funk era produced horn-driven anthems including “Jungle Boogie” (No. 4 pop, 1973) and “Hollywood Swinging” (No. 6 pop and their first No. 1 R&B single, 1974), after they first reached the Hot 100 in 1969 with the instrumental “Kool & the Gang”.
- After adding lead singer James “J.T.” Taylor, the group reached its 1980s commercial peak with “Ladies' Night” (No. 8, 1979), “Too Hot” (No. 5, 1980), “Joanna” (No. 2, 1983), and “Cherish” (No. 2, 1985).
- Their signature song “Celebration” spent two weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in February 1981 and six weeks atop the R&B chart, and remains the group's only No. 1 pop hit.
Sources: Wikipedia — Kool & the Gang; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 and Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs); Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-kool-and-the-gang.html

## KS (Mr Xtortion)
Rapper

- KS, also known as Mr Xtortion, is a Jersey City rapper affiliated with the Black Congress team
- He is documented as a 106 & Park Freestyle Friday champion, known for aggression, bully bars, and freestyles
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-ks-mr-xtortion.html

## Latee
Rapper

- Latee is a Jersey City rapper and an original member of the Flavor Unit, the influential North Jersey hip-hop collective alongside The 45 King, Lakim Shabazz, Chill Rob G, and Queen Latifah.
- He came up in the city's late-1980s scene with fellow Jersey City rappers Apache and Chill Rob G, with whom he formed early ties in high school.
- Working with producer The 45 King, he recorded early Flavor Unit singles including “This Cut's Got Flavor” (1988), among the records that helped establish the crew's sound.
Sources: Wikipedia — Flavor Unit; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-latee.html

## Leonard De Paur
Musician

- Leonard Etienne De Paur (November 18, 1914 -- November 7, 1998) was an American composer, choral director, and arts administrator from Jersey City, New Jersey.
- He directed the De Paur Infantry Chorus during World War II, which toured globally and brought African American choral music to international audiences, and later served in arts administration at Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts.
- His work bridged military service, Black cultural expression, and institutional arts leadership across a career spanning six decades.
Sources: Wikipedia — Leonard De Paur; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-leonard-de-paur.html

## Lil Dev
Rapper

- Lil Dev is a rising rapper from Jersey City, New Jersey, representing the younger generation of the city's hip-hop talent.
- He was documented in the archive's survey of the active urban music scene, signaling his growing presence among local listeners.
- His music typically explores the realities of neighborhood life, delivered with the cadence and style characteristic of modern East Coast rap.
- By establishing his name locally, Lil Dev is actively participating in the continuous evolution of Jersey City's musical identity.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-lil-dev.html

## LilzHP
Artist

- LilzHP is a Jersey City artist documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-lilzhp.html

## Lord Sun Albee Al
MC · Party rocker — 1980s – 1990s

- Lord Sun Albee Al was the founder of the Jersey City crew Sweet Slick and Slide and one of the most revered party-rockers of the city's early hip-hop era.
- Peers describe him as the man who could take over any party, from Jersey City to the Bronx, and credit him with drawing the respect of New York's crews to Chilltown; several artists name him as the reason they began rapping.
- His musical family runs deep in the city: his children are DJ Thunderkatt, the rapper Albee Al, and J-Sass; his nephew is DJ E Double.
- He is remembered throughout the documentary with the refrain "rest in peace" — a measure of his standing as a foundational figure of the scene.
- His children carry the family through the record, by family account given to this archive in 2026: DJ Thunderkatt, the rapper Albee Al, and J-Sass; DJ E Double is their cousin.
- His home block was Stegman Street, remembered by the block's own emcees as an epicenter of hip hop and Black culture in Chilltown Jersey City; a 2022 tribute post by Stegman emcee Young Flizo names him one of the great legends and the home of the Sweet, Slick and Sly Crew, also billed S.S.S.C.
- The crew's hit was It's Magic, named on a period breakin contest flyer that pictures them doing their hit, and by community tribute; the same tribute credits the crew as the first to bring Doug E. Fresh to Jersey City, at the Armory, when he had one single out.
- A period flyer for The Showdown For All DJs at the Benmore Skating Rink on Communipaw Avenue, headlined by Grandmaster Flash imagery, bills Al Bee alongside Strang D, Jimmy M, Sweet S and Sly, Capone, Sir Brown, and the era's crews.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube; Family history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Young Flizo (@youngflizo), Stegman Street Legacy tribute post, Instagram, January 19, 2022 (screenshot preserved in this archive); Period flyers: The Showdown For All DJs (Benmore Skating Rink) and the It's Magic breakin contest flyer, preserved in this archive (see gallery)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-lord-sun-albee-al.html

## Luke Elliot
Musician

- Luke Elliot (born June 30, 1984) is an American musician, producer, and actor whose work draws on American roots music, dark folk, and rock.
- He has released music on independent labels and has built a following in Europe alongside the United States, performing at festivals and developing a reputation for intense, stripped-down live performances.
Sources: Wikipedia — Luke Elliot; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-luke-elliot.html

## MacArthur Munford
Vocalist · Bass singer — 1960 – 2006

- MacArthur J. Munford (January 20, 1943 – September 5, 2006) was a Jersey City soul and doo-wop singer, the bass voice of The Spellbinders
- Born into a working-class Jersey City family, he began in street-corner singing and joined his first group, the Valitons, by 1960 before co-founding the Spellbinders
- In the fall of 1962, while singing with Mark Stevens and the Charmers, Munford was critically injured in an automobile accident on the Lincoln Highway Bridge (Route 1 & 9) heading toward Newark on a stormy night; fellow member Percy Fair and two young women, the Oliver sisters of Bayonne, were killed. The crash ended the group, and Munford spent a long time in critical condition before recovering to co-found the Spellbinders
- Signed to Columbia Records in 1965 under producer Van McCoy, the Spellbinders charted with the Top-25 R&B hit “For You” and “Help Me (Get Myself Back Together Again)”; their album The Magic of the Spellbinders is considered a soul classic, and the group toured with Jackie Wilson, Joe Tex, and Wilson Pickett
- He later formed the a cappella group Vintage with Ray Block of the Valitons, and died of cancer in 2006 at the age of 63
- Through the Van Liew family, Munford is a cornerstone of a six-decade Jersey City musical dynasty: he is a grandson of Fredrick Van Liew — one of the twin brothers, with Sheldon Van Liew, who anchor the family — through his mother, Kathryn Van Liew
- He is the maternal uncle of multi-platinum songwriter Mary Brown (daughter of his sister Lorraine H. Brown), and an elder of the line that reaches Chill Divine (Gary Van Liew) and DJ DX (Robert Van Liew) through Fredrick's twin, Sheldon
Sources: Obituary of MacArthur Munford, Manifesto magazine (2006); Van Liew family history, contributed by Robert Van Liew (DJ DX), The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Interview recollection by Ray Block (the Valitons / Vintage), The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-macarthur-munford.html

## Mali G
Rapper

- Mali G is an artist based in Jersey City, New Jersey, cataloged as part of the city's vibrant and diverse music community.
- His work contributes to the continuous stream of independent releases emerging from Hudson County.
- By participating in the local scene, he helps sustain the creative energy that defines the region's cultural output.
- He navigates the modern musical landscape through digital platforms and community engagement.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mali-g.html

## Maniac Mobb
Hip-hop group — c. 1990s

- Maniac Mobb, also written Maniac Mob, were an underground Jersey City hip hop group of the 1990s, running in the same North Jersey circles as the Flavor Unit.
- Its documented members are Craiga Ski and the late Double J, the Flavor Unit founding member who resurfaced as half of the Mobb, reuniting with DJ Mark the 45 King for production on independent releases.
- The group is named among the era's legends in the community record drawn from the 2006 WizTV documentary review.
- Their surviving recordings include the track Get Up.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Named in the legends record of the WizTV Jersey City DJ Documentary review (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-maniac-mobb.html

## Many Men Records
Record label · Artist collective

- Many Men Records (MMR) is an independent Jersey City hip-hop record label and artist collective founded by the producer and DJ Kamillion, focused on the city's underground rap talent.
- Based in the downtown and urban hubs of Jersey City, the label houses and promotes local artists and anchored a controlled club and party scene in the city.
- Its roster and affiliates include Dibiasi, D-Eastwood, J. Fresh, P.Dot, DJ All Out, and Illanoise, with production led by Kamillion and Illanoise.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Many Men Records roster and label history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-manymen-records.html

## Mariah Lynn
Artist

- Mariah Lynn is a rapper and television personality from Jersey City, New Jersey, known for her appearances on 'Love & Hip Hop: New York' (VH1) and for a catalog of street rap singles released independently.
- She has built a social media following through her blunt, confrontational rap style and her reality television profile, representing a current generation of Jersey City artists who have gained national visibility through cable television.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mariah-lynn.html

## Marilyn McCoo
Musician

- Marilyn McCoo (born September 30, 1943) is an American singer, actress, and television host from Jersey City who became one of the signature voices of the 5th Dimension, the group behind 'Aquarius/Let the Sunshine In' and 'Up, Up and Away'.
- As the group's lead female vocalist, she co-wrote and sang 'You Don't Have to Be a Star (To Be in My Show)', a duet with her husband Billy Davis Jr. that reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1977.
- She hosted the syndicated music television program Solid Gold (1980 to 1983), giving her a second national platform and making her one of the most familiar faces in 1980s pop television.
Sources: Wikipedia — Marilyn McCoo; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-marilyn-mccoo.html

## Mark Cee
DJ · Producer — c. 1990s–2000s

- Mark Cee, also known as Nemo, is a Jersey City DJ and ghost producer who is one of the most influential behind-the-scenes figures in the city's mixtape scene, as documented in the 2006 WizTV documentary.
- He is credited with schooling a generation of Jersey City DJs on the MPC, including DJ DX, making him a key link in the chain of music technology knowledge that passed through the scene.
- He performed as half of the brother duo Double D alongside his brother Shannon, and his final mixtape (around 2000 to 2001) was distributed with the help of DJ BigTime.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mark-cee.html

## Mary Brown
Songwriter · Singer · Producer — 1990s – present

- Mary Brown is a Grammy-nominated singer, songwriter, and producer from Jersey City, New Jersey — among the most decorated writers of the modern R&B era, with 15× RIAA Platinum and 6× RIAA Gold certified credits, a two-time Grammy nominee best known for penning Destiny's Child's first Billboard No. 1, “No, No, No”
- Operatically trained, she won the Governor's Award at the New Jersey Performing Arts Center competition as a teenager, earning a full scholarship to study music at Rutgers University
- During her first year of college she signed to Reprise Records as one-third of Teddy Riley's girl-group trio Abstrac'; the group's single “Right and Hype” spent six weeks on the Billboard Hot 100 and reached No. 23 on the Hot Black Singles chart across a 17-week run
- She went on to session and background work for artists including Brandy, Bobby Brown, Guy, and fellow New Jersey native Whitney Houston
- Her songwriting credits include Destiny's Child's international No. 1 “No, No, No,” work for Jaheim, Day26's debut single “Got Me Goin’,” and Danity Kane's “Bad Girl” (featuring Missy Elliott) and “Ecstasy” (featuring Rick Ross) — the last two written on MTV's Making the Band 3, where Sean “Diddy” Combs praised her “mean pen game”
- She contributed to Michael Jackson's final studio album, Invincible (2001), and has worked with Mary J. Blige, Wyclef Jean, Queen Latifah, Usher, SWV, Patti LaBelle, Diana Ross, and LL Cool J; her “No, No, No” was later sampled by Jack Harlow on his Grammy-nominated Come Home the Kids Miss You (2022)
- Her honors include Soul Train's Lady of Soul Award and four BMI Publisher & Songwriter Awards; she co-chaired the Recording Academy's New York Songwriters & Composers Wing (2022–2023), runs the publishing company Ms. Mary's Music, and works as an educator and philanthropist
- She is a granddaughter of Kathryn Van Liew and, with her brother Cool Sir Brown, one of the family's modern hitmakers; she is the niece of soul singer MacArthur Munford of the Spellbinders — her mother, Lorraine H. Brown, was Munford's sister — placing her among the cornerstone figures of the Van Liew musical dynasty alongside Chill Divine (Gary Van Liew) and DJ DX (Robert Van Liew)
- By her account in the Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary, her arranging gift showed early: as a teenager she wrote and arranged the backing harmonies that helped her brother Cool Sir Brown win an MC battle at P.S. 11, and her earliest studio work came through the family's friendship with Biz Markie and Doug E. Fresh.
Sources: Mary Brown — artist biography and press materials, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; RIAA Gold & Platinum database; “No, No, No” (Destiny's Child) — songwriting credit; Van Liew / Brown family history, contributed by Robert Van Liew (DJ DX), The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mary-brown.html

## Maude Roberts George
Musician

- Maude Roberts George (September 27, 1888 – December 1, 1943) was an American singer, arts administrator, and music critic.
- She was president of the National Association of Negro Musicians (NANM) from 1933 to 1935.
- She was also president of the Chicago Music Association, and a music critic for The Chicago Defender newspaper.
Sources: Wikipedia — Maude Roberts George; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-maude-roberts-george.html

## Maury Yeston
Musician

- Maury Yeston (born October 23, 1945) is an American composer, lyricist and music theorist.
- Yeston has written the music and lyrics for several Broadway musicals and is also a classical orchestral and ballet composer.
- Among his Broadway musicals are Nine in 1982, Titanic in 1997, for both of which he won Tony Awards for Best Musical and Best Score and was nominated for Grammy Awards, and Grand Hotel in 1989, for which he was nominated for a Tony Award for best score and two Drama Desk Awards for his music and lyrics.
Sources: Wikipedia — Maury Yeston; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-maury-yeston.html

## Max YB
Rapper

- Max YB is a Jersey City rapper representing the Curries Woods projects and the Uptop section
- He appears alongside DJ Swill B in the city's recent hip-hop catalog and is documented for versatility, energy, hunger, and passion
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-max-yb.html

## Mel Odoms
Producer · Label founder · Songwriter — c. 1981 – 1990s

- Mel Odoms, born Melvin Odoms, was a producer, songwriter, and founder of the Jersey City label Mel-O Records, active in the city's music scene of the 1980s and early 1990s.
- Per his Discogs artist profile, he was a college track star and a sometime contract employee for Kool & the Gang, and he worked with many Jersey City musicians to get the funk and soul group Tomorrow's Edition their first records released, including the single "U Turn Me On" (CBS, 1981) and the album A Song for Everyone (Atlantic, 1982), produced by Amir Bayyan of the Kool & the Gang family.
- He founded the independent label Mel-O Records, which in 1990 released the debut single of Jersey City rap group Double XX Posse, "Executive Class" backed with "On A Mission," carrying his production credit as Mel-O Odoms.
- His Discogs profile, citing newspaper coverage, records that he was shot by a police officer and received a substantial financial award as a result.
Sources: Mel Odoms, Discogs artist profile; Double XX Posse, Executive Class / On A Mission (Mel-O, 1990), Discogs; Tomorrow's Edition, A Song for Everyone (Atlantic 80009-1, 1982), Soul and Funk Music
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mel-odoms.html

## Mel-O Records
Record label — 1981 – 1990

- Mel-O Records was an independent Jersey City record label founded by producer Mel Odoms; its Discogs label page lists its contact as Jersey City, NJ, and states it was founded to release Tomorrow's Edition records.
- The label pressed its address onto its records: 94 Union Street, Jersey City, NJ 07304, with a 201 phone number, placing its operation in the city's Bergen-Lafayette section.
- Its catalog ran from 1981 to 1990: Tomorrow's Edition's "U Turn Me On" (MLO-437, 1981), "I'm The Best" (MLO-438, 1984), and "Million Dollars" (MLO 800 and MLO 900, 1987), plus "Things Ain't As Bad As They Seem" (MLO 777, 1985), a promo 12-inch by an act billed simply as Jersey, written by K. Nance and D. Sanchez and produced by Amir Bayyan, Adam Bayyan, and Dave Dastard for Mel-O Productions, with Melvin Odoms as executive producer.
- Its final and best-known release was the debut single of Jersey City rap group Double XX Posse, "Executive Class" backed with "On A Mission" (MLO-1100, 1990), with production credited to Mel-O Odoms and Ray Howell; Big Beat reissued it in 1991 and signed the group, who reached No. 1 on Billboard's Hot Rap Singles in 1992.
- By the firsthand account of DJ Flash, given to this archive in 2026, the "Executive Class" session drew on a room of Jersey City DJs, including Flash on drums, DJ Stranger Dee on the sample, DJ Wimpy Bee producing, and brothers Sugar Chris and Magic on the bassline.
Sources: Mel-o Records, Discogs label page (Jersey City, NJ); Jersey, Things Ain't As Bad As They Seem (MLO 777, 1985), label credits and Jersey City address, Discogs; Double XX Posse, Executive Class / On A Mission (Mel-O 1100, 1990), Discogs; Mel Odoms, Discogs artist profile (label founder); Oral history: DJ Flash, as told to The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mel-o-records.html

## Midnite The DJ
DJ

- Midnite The DJ is a Jersey City-area open-format DJ who works weddings, festivals, clubs, corporate events, and cruises across New Jersey and New York, with travel dates beyond the region.
- He bills as an award-winning DJ and performs under the motto 'If it ain't Nite, it ain't right'.
- He is connected to the city's broader DJ community, with peers including DJ Madden and WizTV among his Instagram followers.
Sources: Instagram @midnitethedj — profile and professional billing; Official site — midnitethedj.com
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-midnite-the-dj.html

## Mimi Jones
Musician

- Mimi Jones (born March 25, 1972) is an American bassist, vocalist, composer, bandleader, and educator, and the founder of Hot Tone Music, her independent label and production company.
- She has performed and recorded with a wide range of jazz and creative music artists, building a catalog as a bandleader that draws on jazz, soul, and global music traditions.
- As an educator she has taught at workshops and institutions across the United States and internationally, and has been a visible advocate for women in jazz.
Sources: Wikipedia — Mimi Jones; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mimi-jones.html

## Mista Quietman
DJ · Remixer — c. early 1990s – present

- Mista Quietman (Antwan Anderson) is a Jersey City DJ, blend-tape producer, and remixer whose documented work runs from the city's 1990s mixtape era to today, and a key figure in preserving the scene's history online.
- A member of the Brick Bandits, the club-music collective, he was by his own account among those who carried Baltimore club, and later Newark’s Brick City club — the sound that became Jersey club — across the Hudson into Hudson County.
- His tape catalog, preserved in his personal archive, includes The Intimate Thoughts of Mista Quiet Man (Club Traxx), Mista Quiet Man's Birthday Bash, Now That's What I Call Blenden', and The Past Versus the Present Artist, with covers by the Harlem mixtape shop Pro Art and Jersey City's own Blaze-In-Arts.
- The Club Traxx shout-outs are a primary document of the 1990s scene: he thanks the staff of Albert's, which he calls one of the hottest night clubs in Jersey City, naming Donald "Wiz" Davis, DJ Trever-D, and Wimpy-B, along with Omar X and Isaac of Supreme Production and the Friday nights at Mahogany's.
- Through the late 2000s and 2010s he ran a prolific Jersey club CD series under the Jersey Club Kingz banner, mixing the club scene's producers, Jayhood, Sliink, Nadus, Taj, Frosty, Tim Dolla, and the rest, for the 201; the covers carry his own remixes, including a Crank Dat Rudeboy remix with DJ J-Money and his Tip Toe extended blend, a co hosted volume with Ms. Porsh, a tribute volume for Baltimore's K-Swift after her passing in 2008, and even a kids' edition of club edits.
- He produced 'The 201 High School Anthem', a Jersey City rework of DJ Sliink's 'High School Anthem', calling out Lincoln, Ferris, Dickinson, and Snyder high schools -- a local anthem of the city's school-era DJ culture.
- His SoundCloud catalog dating to 2012 includes Jersey club loops and club mixes such as “The Sharpbounce 2ktwelve” and “C4 Rock.”
- He runs The Official Mista Quietman Channel on YouTube, where he has preserved the full upload of WizTV’s Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006), making one of the most important documents in Jersey City mixtape-culture history accessible to the public.
- He is listed as a supporter in the TTE Edits Bandcamp community, connecting the Jersey City scene to the wider Jersey club editing world.
- His more recent work moves into house: his Bandcamp collects house remixes, among them a rework of the Blue Magic classic “Spell On My Mind” that, by his account, reached rotation on DJ Qua’s Friday show on WBLS.
Sources: The 201 High School Anthem (SoundCloud); Instagram @mistaquietman; SoundCloud catalog; The Official Mista Quietman Channel — documentary upload (YouTube); Mista Quietman personal archive: tape covers and photographs, shared with The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (see galleries)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mista-quietman.html

## Mixed Company
A cappella group · Vocal group — 1982 – 1993

- Mixed Company was an a cappella and street-corner vocal quintet formed in 1982 on Henry Street in Jersey City, blending doo-wop, Motown, rock, and rhythm and blues.
- The group came together after the breakup of the Jersey City street-corner outfit the Street-Tones (later Patty and the Street-Tones), and took its name to reflect its mix of nationalities, genders, and personalities.
- The 1982 lineup was Patty Zielinski, John “Bull” Zielinski, Jimmy Woods, Jeffrey Chambers, and Charles “Cada” Brooks on bass vocals; they sang together until 1993, when they added instrumentation and became Slick and Soul.
- For Jersey City's Catamount Records they cut a now sought-after 45, a cover of Leiber and Stoller's “Kansas City” backed with Barbara Mason's “Yes, I'm Ready.”
- Their album Buzzin' With Mixed Company paired classic doo-wop with country and down-home R&B, and their catalog was later reissued in officially remastered digital form.
- Performing over the decades as The Original Mixed Company out of nearby Bayonne, members shared stages with national oldies acts including The Drifters, The Marvelettes, and The Platters.
Sources: Group history and discography contributed to The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mixed-company.html

## Money Train Entertainment
Entertainment company · Event production · Artist management

- Money Train Entertainment is a Jersey City media, event-production, and artist-management collective founded by entrepreneur and marketing executive Shakim Thompson.
- Rooted in the nightlife culture of Jersey City and the wider North Jersey and New York metropolitan area, the company built a reputation for dominating regional street promotion, growing from high-energy parties across the city into a full-scale entertainment network.
- Money Train hosted the Winter Blaze DJ Battle — the area’s first premier seasonal turntablist competition — pitting elite regional DJs against each other live.
- Thompson expanded the brand into media with a music-and-lifestyle television show on Channel 51 (Comcast) reaching some 2.5 million residents; filmed and directed by WizTV, it featured major recording artists while spotlighting the turntablism of DJ Wizard and the blends of DJ DX, bridging street culture and mainstream television.
Sources: Money Train Events — Instagram (@moneytrainevents); Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-money-train-entertainment.html

## Montana Dess
Rapper

- Montana Dess is a musical artist from Jersey City, New Jersey, identified in the archive's catalog of the active scene.
- His output adds to the continuous flow of original music being produced in Hudson County's underground.
- He operates within the independent music ecosystem, relying on community support and digital distribution.
- His inclusion in the archive reflects the ongoing effort to document the working artists of the current era.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-montana-dess.html

## Mr. Chicken
Artist

- Mr. Chicken is a Jersey City artist documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mr-chicken.html

## Mr. Low Kash 'N Da Shady Bunch
Rap group · MC — 1990s

- Mr. Low Kash 'N Da Shady Bunch were a Jersey City hip-hop act of the mid-1990s, part of the city's boom-bap rap generation.
- Their album Forever Raw was originally issued in 1996; its catalog survives on streaming, with tracks including “Recognize The Low,” “Heatholder's,” “The Best At This,” and “Down With Me.”
- In 2022 Forever Raw was reissued on vinyl for the first time by Most Wanted Records as a numbered, limited double LP, the listing tagging the group's origin as Jersey City, New Jersey.
Sources: Mr. Low Kash 'N Da Shady Bunch — Spotify; Forever Raw (1996 / 2022 reissue, Jersey City NJ) — Most Wanted Records; Mr. Low Kash 'N Da Shady Bunch — Discogs
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mr-low-kash.html

## MrCashedOut
Rapper

- MrCashedOut is a Jersey City musical artist who has carved out a space within the city's active entertainment community.
- His moniker reflects a focus on ambition and success, common themes in contemporary urban music and hip-hop.
- He was identified during the archive's survey, highlighting his visibility among local peers and audiences.
- He continues to release music and build his brand independently within the Hudson County area.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mrcashedout.html

## MVP
Artist

- MVP is a Jersey City artist documented in the archive's ongoing survey of the city's music scene.
- This entry has been opened and awaits biographical detail, era, and receipts from the community.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-mvp.html

## Nancy Sinatra
Musician

- Nancy Sandra Sinatra (born June 8, 1940) is an American singer, actress, film producer, and author.
- She is the elder daughter of Frank Sinatra and Nancy Sinatra (née Barbato) and is known for her 1966 signature hit "These Boots Are Made for Walkin'".
- Nancy Sinatra began her career as a singer in November 1957 with an appearance on her father's ABC television variety series The Frank Sinatra Show, but initially achieved success only in Europe and Japan.
Sources: Wikipedia — Nancy Sinatra; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-nancy-sinatra.html

## Nero
Rapper · Host · Media personality

- Nero, also known as Nero CKP, is a Jersey City hip-hop artist, event host, and media personality active across the city's local music scene.
- He hosts the From Da Mud (FDM) Thugcast, a hip-hop podcast where he interviews Jersey City legends, hip-hop pioneers, and up-and-coming talent — building an ongoing oral record of the city's scene.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (From Da Mud / FDM Thugcast; Instagram @Nero CKP)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-nero-ckp.html

## New Jersey Mix Kings 201
DJ collective

- New Jersey Mix Kings 201 is a New Jersey DJ collective in the regional mixtape and blends tradition — mashups, custom remixes, and a cappella blends of classic hip-hop.
- Its members include DJ Hazz B, DJ Nyce Riley, DJ Junebugg, DJ Flash Hamilton, and Semaj da DJ.
Sources: Community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (surfaced via search; pending verification)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-new-jersey-mix-kings-201.html

## Nina Foxx
Vocalist

- Nina Foxx is a creative force in the Jersey City music scene, documented in the archive's survey of active artists.
- Her artistic vision contributes to the diverse array of sounds emerging from the city's independent networks.
- She leverages digital platforms to share her work, navigating the modern landscape of independent music production.
- Her presence ensures that female voices in the local underground are properly recognized and recorded.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-nina-foxx.html

## Norman Edge
Musician

- Norman Edge (April 29, 1934 -- June 4, 2018) was an American jazz double bassist from Jersey City, New Jersey, who became a reliable fixture in the New York metropolitan jazz scene.
- Throughout his career spanning several decades, he provided the rhythmic foundation for numerous ensembles, demonstrating the high level of musicianship characteristic of Jersey City's jazz exports.
- His work in small groups and larger bands helped sustain the vibrant live jazz culture of the mid-to-late twentieth century.
- He is remembered as a dedicated craftsman of the double bass, contributing to the rich tradition of jazz instrumentalists hailing from Hudson County.
Sources: Wikipedia — Norman Edge; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-norman-edge.html

## Omar X
Scene figure · Producer

- Omar X is a figure of Jersey City's founding hip hop generation, remembered with Supreme Production; Mista Quietman's 1990s Club Traxx shout-outs big him up alongside Isaac of Supreme Production.
- He stands in the Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait by Tijon Maro, preserved in this archive.
Sources: Mista Quietman, Club Traxx shout-outs (primary artifact, see his gallery); Jersey City Hip Hop Legends portrait, Tijon Maro (see DJ Flash's gallery)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-omar-x.html

## Orlando Wharton
Executive · A&R · Manager

- Orlando Wharton is a music executive who came up through Jersey City's early-2000s scene, beginning his career managing his close friends — the rappers Ransom and Hitchcock of the duo The A-Team.
- Known in that era as "Orlando" or "Whartonberg," he built the Presidential Crew and its production arm, Presidential Beats, around the city's independent mixtape run.
- After Ransom's incarceration and the death of affiliate Stack Bundles, Wharton stepped away from music before returning as an A&R executive — rising to Executive Vice President at Capitol Music Group and President of Priority Records.
- As an A&R he is credited with discovering and signing a generation of stars, including Kodak Black, Fetty Wap, A Boogie Wit Da Hoodie, and XXXTentacion.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Industry and community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-orlando-wharton.html

## Overlake
Musical group

- Overlake is a critically acclaimed shoegaze and indie rock trio based in Jersey City, New Jersey.
- They are known for their expansive, wall-of-sound approach, combining heavily affected guitars with dreamy, melodic vocal deliveries.
- Their music connects the local Jersey City rock underground to the wider national shoegaze revival of the 2010s and 2020s, earning praise from independent music press.
- By producing immersive, atmospheric rock, Overlake has established themselves as one of the most distinctive acts to emerge from the city's modern indie scene.
Sources: Wikipedia — Overlake; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-overlake.html

## Ozzie Nelson
Bandleader · Musician — 1930s to 1940s

- Ozzie Nelson was born in Jersey City on March 20, 1906, and became one of the most popular American bandleaders of the 1930s.
- His orchestra recorded a string of national hits in the 1930s, including “And Then Some,” reported as a number 1 record in 1935 in the era before the Billboard singles chart existed (retrospective ranking per Joel Whitburn's Pop Memories).
- He later created and starred in the radio and television series The Adventures of Ozzie and Harriet, and was the father of the rock and roll singer Ricky Nelson.
Sources: Wikipedia — Ozzie Nelson; Joel Whitburn, Pop Memories 1890 to 1954 (retrospective chart reconstruction)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-ozzie-nelson.html

## P.Dot
Rapper

- P.Dot is a Jersey City hip-hop artist on the Many Men Records roster.
- This entry has been opened and awaits fuller biographical detail and receipts from the community.
Sources: Many Men Records roster and label history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-p-dot.html

## P.M. Dawn
Musical group

- PM Dawn is a hip-hop duo from Jersey City, New Jersey, consisting of brothers Attrell Cordes (Prince Be) and Jarrett Cordes (DJ Minutemix), who blended hip-hop, soul, psychedelia, and new age into a style entirely their own.
- Their debut single 'Set Adrift on Memory Bliss' (1991, Gee Street/Island Records) sampled Spandau Ballet's 'True' and reached No. 1 in the United Kingdom and No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in the United States, giving Jersey City one of its most commercially successful chart-toppers.
- Their debut album 'Of the Heart, of the Soul and of the Cross: The Utopian Experience' (1991) established them as a critical darling of the alternative hip-hop and new jack swing era.
Sources: Wikipedia — P.M. Dawn; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-pm-dawn.html

## Paul Banks
Musician

- Paul Banks is an American jazz pianist from Jersey City, New Jersey, who has established a solid reputation within the New York metropolitan jazz scene.
- His playing draws on the deep traditions of acoustic jazz, demonstrating the technical proficiency required to compete in one of the world's most demanding musical environments.
- He has performed and recorded in various ensemble settings, contributing his harmonic knowledge and rhythmic sensitivity to numerous projects.
- Banks continues the legacy of highly skilled jazz instrumentalists emerging from Hudson County to work across the river.
Sources: Wikipedia — Paul Banks; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-paul-banks.html

## Peter Lemongello
Musician

- Peter Lemongello (born February 11, 1947) is an American singer from Jersey City, New Jersey, known for his self-financed double album 'Love '76', which he marketed through television advertising in the mid-1970s -- one of the earlier examples of direct-response music marketing in the United States.
- His television campaign made 'Love '76' a commercially successful release and established him as a recognizable figure in New Jersey entertainment circles during the disco era.
Sources: Wikipedia — Peter Lemongello; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-peter-lemongello.html

## Peter Sliker
Musician

- Peter Sliker (June 7, 1925 – June 25, 2010) was an American operatic bass-baritone who had a lengthy career performing at the Metropolitan Opera from 1961 through 1989.
- He specialized in portraying comprimario roles and was admired for his comic timing.
- He has appeared on several Live from the Metropolitan Opera broadcasts and Metropolitan Opera radio broadcasts.
Sources: Wikipedia — Peter Sliker; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-peter-sliker.html

## Phil Urso
Musician

- Phil Urso (2 October 1925, Jersey City, New Jersey — 7 April 2008, Denver, Colorado) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and composer known for his association with trumpeter Chet Baker.
- Urso learned clarinet as a child and switched to tenor sax while in high school.
- He served in the Navy during World War II and then moved to New York City in 1947.
Sources: Wikipedia — Phil Urso; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-phil-urso.html

## Philip James
Musician

- Philip Frederick Wright James (May 17, 1890 -- November 1, 1975) was an American composer, conductor, and music educator born in Jersey City, New Jersey.
- He chaired the music department at New York University for many years and composed works for orchestra, chamber ensemble, and voice, with several pieces performed by major American orchestras during the mid-twentieth century.
Sources: Wikipedia — Philip James; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-philip-james.html

## Phyllis Newman
Musician

- Phyllis Newman (March 19, 1933 -- September 15, 2019) was an American actress and singer from Jersey City, New Jersey, who won the 1962 Tony Award for Best Featured Actress in a Musical for her role as Martha Vail in 'Subways Are for Sleeping' on Broadway.
- She received the Isabelle Stevenson Award from the Tonys in 2009, a second nomination for 'Broadway Bound' (1987), and two Drama Desk nominations, building one of the more decorated Broadway careers of her generation.
- She was also a television personality and activist, co-founding the Phyllis Newman Women's Health Initiative to support women in the theater industry facing serious illness.
Sources: Wikipedia — Phyllis Newman; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-phyllis-newman.html

## Positively Black
Hip-hop group — 1988 – 1990s

- Positively Black were a Jersey City conscious-rap group formed in 1988, built around a message of Black consciousness that set them apart from the city's other crews.
- The group was led by Prince Kyrie with King N and the DJs Storm and Tyrell, and was managed by Youth Champagne, remembered as one of the city's first female managers and rappers; founding member Salam is remembered by the group, rest in peace.
- They recorded for the family-run Trumpet Records before signing to Select Records, sharing a roster era with acts such as Chubb Rock, Kid 'n Play, and UTFO.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-positively-black.html

## Presidential
Crew · Management circle — early 2000s

- Presidential — also known as the Presidential Crew or Presidential Blends — was the street crew, management circle, and inner circle built by Orlando Wharton around the rappers Ransom and Hitchcock and their close Jersey City associates during the city's independent mixtape run.
- Its production arm was Presidential Beats, the beat-making team of Producer Mayor and DJ Nel E Nel.
Sources: Industry and community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-presidential-crew.html

## Presidential Beats
Production company

- Presidential Beats is a Jersey City production team and the beat-making arm of the Presidential Crew, Orlando Wharton's Jersey City circle.
- Its production core paired Producer Mayor with DJ Nel E Nel, supplying beats to the city's rap scene.
Sources: Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-presidential-beats.html

## PressureOnline
Artist · Media

- PressureOnline is a Jersey City artist and media figure representing the Curries Woods projects
- He is documented for lyrical ability, aggression, work output, and punchlines, and operates the outlet pressureonline.com
Sources: PressureOnline — official site; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-pressureonline.html

## Prince Kyrie
MC — 1988 – present

- Prince Kyrie is a Jersey City MC and the founder and frontman of the conscious-rap group Positively Black, which he started in 1988.
- He recalls the group's aim of raising Black consciousness at a time when few Jersey City acts took that path, and its journey from the family-run Trumpet Records to Select Records.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-prince-kyrie.html

## Producer Mayor
Producer

- Producer Mayor — also known as MayoR or MayoR LLC4 — is a hip-hop beatmaker from Jersey City who frequently collaborates with the rapper Ransom.
- He produced Ransom's 2022 collaborative EP This Life Made Me and contributed tracks to No Rest for the Wicked.
- He is part of the Presidential Beats production team alongside DJ Nel E Nel, under Orlando Wharton's Presidential.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (Ransom — This Life Made Me, 2022; No Rest for the Wicked); Instagram @MayoR LLC4; Community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-producer-mayor.html

## Ransom
Rapper — c. late 1990s–present

- Ransom (Randy Nicholls) is a Jersey City rapper who moved to the city around age eight and built his reputation as one of the most technically precise MCs on the New Jersey mixtape circuit.
- He is one half of the A-Team alongside Hitchcock, a duo affiliated with DJ Clue's Desert Storm alongside Fabolous and Stack Bundles.
- The A-Team released the Hardhood Classics mixtape trilogy, earning a reputation for punishing punchlines and grimy street verses that defined the era's bar-for-bar standard.
- After a Def Jam deal did not materialize the A-Team split around 2006; Ransom has continued a prolific solo career.
- In the A-Team era the duo was managed by Orlando Wharton — later a Capitol Music Group EVP and president of Priority Records — as part of his Presidential Crew.
Sources: AllMusic — Ransom biography; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Industry and community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-ransom.html

## Revenue Wrong
Rapper

- Revenue Wrong, also known as Wrong, is a Jersey City rapper from Arlington Park affiliated with the Blockstar and Frontline teams.
- He is remembered as a 'legend before the internet' -- a figure whose reputation was built entirely through in-person performance and word of mouth before digital platforms could have carried it.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community tributes comments (2026); confirmed by archive, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-revenue-wrong.html

## Richie Havens
Musician

- Richard Pierce Havens (January 21, 1941 -- April 22, 2013) was an American singer-songwriter and guitarist known for his distinctive open-tuning guitar style and a voice that bridged folk, soul, and rhythm and blues.
- He is best remembered for his opening performance at Woodstock in 1969, where his improvised extended set -- including the a cappella spiritual 'Freedom' -- became one of the defining moments of the festival.
- His catalog spans more than twenty albums from the mid-1960s through the 2000s, and his covers of Bob Dylan and The Beatles became near-definitive readings for a generation of listeners.
Sources: Wikipedia — Richie Havens; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-richie-havens.html

## Riggamortis
Rapper

- Riggamortis, born James Hamilton and also known as Jigga Brown, was a legendary Jersey City underground hip-hop artist, a revered MC known for fierce, rapid-fire bars and a staple of the city's rap and Alumni scene before his passing.
- He is credited by community voices with sharpening a generation of MCs: 'the man that made me perfect my punchlines,' as one tribute put it.
- DJ Flash, in a firsthand account given to this archive in 2026, states that he and Riggamortis were first cousins and that the 1950s Jersey City R&B star Roy Hamilton was their great-great-grandfather.
- Born James Hamilton, he shared the Hamilton family name with his first cousin DJ Flash, who records as DJ Flash Hamilton, of the family line that names Roy Hamilton as its forebear.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Community tributes comments (2026); confirmed by archive, 2026; Riggamortis Freestyle (Jigga Ho) in Jersey City, YouTube; Riggamortis, The Greatest That Ever Did It!, Kenny Kenn channel, YouTube; Tribute reel, Instagram; Family account (real name, billing), given to this archive, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-rigga-mortis.html

## Rob Mazurek
Musician

- Rob Mazurek (born July 8, 1965) is an American composer, cornetist, and visual artist whose work moves across jazz, avant-garde, and experimental music.
- He has led the Chicago Underground projects and Exploding Star Orchestra, and his work has been released on Thrill Jockey and other leading avant-garde labels, making him one of the more internationally prominent experimental musicians with Jersey City connections.
Sources: Wikipedia — Rob Mazurek; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-rob-mazurek.html

## Robert "Kool" Bell
Musician

- Robert Earl 'Kool' Bell (born October 8, 1950), also known as Muhammad Bayyan, is an American musician, singer, and songwriter who grew up in Jersey City, New Jersey.
- He co-founded Kool & the Gang in Jersey City in 1964 alongside his brother Ronald Bell and five neighborhood friends, all of whom attended Lincoln High School in the city.
- As the band's bassist and a driving creative force, he helped build one of the most sampled catalogs in music history, spanning funk, soul, R&B, and pop across six decades.
- The section of Maple Street in Jersey City where Bell and his bandmates grew up was officially renamed 'Kool & the Gang Way' in 2016 in their honor.
Sources: Wikipedia — Robert "Kool" Bell; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-robert-kool-bell.html

## Ronnie & the Hi-Lites
Musical group

- Ronnie & The Hi-Lites were an American doo-wop musical group, formed in Jersey City, New Jersey, United States, in the early 1960s.
- The group was originally called the Cascades, not to be confused with a different group of the same name.
- Initially consisting of tenors Sonny Caldwell and John Whitney, bass singer Kenny Overby and baritone Stanley Brown, the group added 12-year-old Ronnie Goodson as their lead singer.
- Their single “I Wish That We Were Married” (1962) reached No. 16 on the Billboard Hot 100.
Sources: Wikipedia — Ronnie & the Hi-Lites; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-ronnie-and-the-hi-lites.html

## Roy Hamilton
Singer — 1954 to 1969

- Roy Hamilton was raised in Jersey City from his early teens, after his family moved from Leesburg, Georgia around 1943, and he was discovered performing in the city.
- He sang in Jersey City church choirs with the Searchlight Gospel Singers and boxed Golden Gloves before signing with Epic Records, where he became the label's first star.
- His recording of “You'll Never Walk Alone” spent eight weeks at number 1 on the Billboard R&B chart in 1954.
- “Unchained Melody” reached number 1 R&B and the pop top 10 in 1955, and “Don't Let Go” reached number 13 pop in 1958, a record often cited as the first rock and roll hit recorded in stereo.
- “You Can Have Her” reached number 12 pop in 1961. Hamilton was a documented influence on Elvis Presley.
- He died in 1969 at the age of 40.
- By family account, given to this archive in 2026 by DJ Flash, Hamilton's line runs into the city's hip-hop era: DJ Flash, who records as DJ Flash Hamilton and is credited with originating Jersey City's mixtape scene, and the late rapper Riggamortis, first cousins, name him as their great-great-grandfather.
Sources: Wikipedia — Roy Hamilton; Billboard chart history (Hot R&B and Hot 100)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-roy-hamilton.html

## Ruga
Rapper

- Ruga is a Jersey City rapper affiliated with the Team Fetti and Section 8 crews
- He is documented as a BET 106 & Park Freestyle Friday champion and as undefeated in the JC Hunger Games battle league, known for metaphors and punchlines
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-ruga.html

## Rye Coalition
Musical group

- Rye Coalition is a post-hardcore band based in Jersey City, New Jersey, United States.
- The band has released four albums, three EPs, a split 12-inch with Karp, and several singles.
- Described by member David Leto as "a loud rock and roll band with obvious punk influences and many classic rock influences", Rye Coalition has often been associated with genres such as emo and post-hardcore.
Sources: Wikipedia — Rye Coalition; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-rye-coalition.html

## Semaj da DJ
DJ — c. 1998–present

- Semaj da DJ — whose name is James spelled backward — is a Jersey City DJ who has been mixing since 1998, coming up by studying the local DJs around him and mastering the craft of mixing and blending.
- He founded his own label, T.O.M. Digital Production — also known as Team of Monstars — a collective of close friends who shared his interest in the business, among them DJ Evil, E Baby, Yoshi, and Shady.
- His first mixtape reached stores in early 2000; as his fan base grew he expanded the team and his retail footprint — operating record stores in West New York and Union City and reaching into other cities — and he sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records alongside the city's top DJs.
- That visibility, amplified by the rise of social media, led a local filmmaker to include him in the 2006 Jersey City DJ Documentary.
- He later released the Jersey City Mix, which went viral on the strength of a defiant hometown catchphrase — spreading across boomboxes, ringtones, and passing cars to become one of the city's most repeated hooks.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006); Instagram @semajdadj — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Artist biography, provided by Semaj da DJ, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-semaj.html

## Shakim Thompson
Entrepreneur · Promoter · Executive

- Shakim Thompson is a Jersey City entrepreneur and marketing executive, and the founder of Money Train Entertainment.
- Under his leadership Money Train grew from throwing parties across the city into a full-scale regional entertainment, promotion, and media network — hosting the Winter Blaze DJ Battle and launching a Channel 51 (Comcast) television show filmed by WizTV.
Sources: Shakim Thompson — Instagram (@moneytrainsha); Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-shakim-thompson.html

## Skip Jackson
Record producer · Arranger · Singer — 1950s – 1982

- Skip Jackson — born Tommy Brown — was a Jersey City singer, arranger, and producer who wrote and produced much of the catalog of Stan Krause's Catamount Records.
- He came up in the doo-wop era with the Shantons, neighborhood friends from Jersey City who began singing together in basements and at local dances; by his biographer's account he learned piano as a boy on a discarded instrument in the coal bin where his father worked as a building manager.
- Through the 1970s he was the musical hand behind much of Catamount's output, arranging and producing the label's sides and, on some records, supplying the instrumentation and most of the vocals himself.
- Under his own name he recorded as Skip Jackson & the Natural Experience, whose “Micro Wave Boogie” (Catamount, 1979) headlined the label's disco-era series.
- His Catamount arrangement and production credits include 14 Karat Soul's “Doo-Wopp Disco” and “Please Say You Want Me,” the Catamounts' “Creation of Love,” the Metro-Liners, the Bonaires, and Greenville's “Just Got To Party.”
- He continued making records with Stan Krause until his death in 1982.
Sources: Skip Jackson — Sir Shambling's Deep Soul Heaven; Catamount Records — Classic Urban Harmony; Skip Jackson discography — Discogs
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-skip-jackson.html

## Sonny Igoe
Musician

- Owen Joseph "Sonny" Igoe (October 8, 1923 – March 28, 2012) was an American jazz drummer and music educator who, toured with the orchestras of Tommy Reed (1913–2012), Les Elgart, Ina Ray Hutton, Benny Goodman, and Woody Herman from the mid-1940s to the mid-1950s.
- From the mid-1940s to 1988, he performed on over 79 recordings with bands and artists, including The Buddy Stewart (1922–1950) Quintet, Benny Goodman and His Orchestra, Woody Herman and His Orchestra, Frances Wayne with Neal Hefti and His Orchestra, Rita Moss with the George Williams Orchestra, Charlie Ventura, Tony Bennett, Billy Maxted and His Manhattan Jazz Band, The Chuck Wayne Quintet, The Don Elliott Quintet, Joe Wilder, Phil Napoleon and His…
Sources: Wikipedia — Sonny Igoe; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-sonny-igoe.html

## Soul Shades
Duo

- Soul Shades is a duo built around Jersey City’s DJ DX — who handles turntablism, rap, and production — alongside multi-instrumentalist Julie Schatz on piano and violin, the two trading between the decks and live instrumentation.
- Their breakout — a mashup of Nimino’s “I Only Smoke When I Drink” with Sting’s “Englishman in New York” — drew more than 10 million views across TikTok and Instagram, including 5 million on Ninja Tune’s own TikTok, and was publicly co-signed by Nimino himself; Ninja Tune and its Counter Records imprint posted it in late 2024, crediting Soul Shades.
- The duo went on to further success with their release “Buzz In London.”
Sources: Oral history: Robert Van Liew (DJ DX), The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Soul Shades remix credited by Ninja Tune / Counter Records (Instagram, Nov 8 2024); Soul Shades Music on TikTok (@soulshadesmusic); Soul Shades Music — official site
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-soul-shades.html

## Speedy Baby
DJ

- Speedy Baby is a Jersey City artist whose work was documented during the archive's cataloging of the active music scene.
- They bring their distinct style to the diverse array of independent music being created in the city.
- Operating outside the major label system, they connect with listeners through grassroots and digital networks.
- They are part of the vibrant, foundational layer of creators that keep the local arts community thriving.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-speedy-baby.html

## Spent
Musical group
