- Spent was an American indie rock band from Jersey City, New Jersey, consisting of singer/guitarist John King, guitarist/keyboardist Annie Hayden, bassist Joe Weston, and drummer Ed Radich.
- They released albums on Thrill Jockey Records through the 1990s and were associated with the downtown New York and Jersey City indie rock scenes of that era.
Sources: Wikipedia — Spent; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-spent.html

## Square Records
Record label

- Square Records is a Jersey City record label founded by Stan Krause, associated with his Stan's Square Records store and his Catamount Records label
- The label released Chill Divine's 1990 hip-hop 12-inch, “Puttin' It Wildly / Long Live the Lyrical,” connecting the Krause record-store lineage to the city's early hip-hop era
Sources: Discogs — Chill Divine, Puttin' It Wildly / Long Live The Lyrical (Square Records, 1990); Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; Oral history: The Jersey City Sound, 2026 (Square Records founded by Stan Krause)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-square-records.html

## Stan Krause
Record producer · Record store owner · Label founder — 1964 to 2016

- Stan Krause was the Jersey City record store owner, record producer, and label founder behind Stan's Square Records and Catamount Records, a central figure in the city's acappella and rhythm-and-blues history.
- He founded Catamount Records in 1964 and opened Stan's Square Records in 1965, and the label launched the recording careers of vocal groups including The Persuasions and 14 Karat Soul.
- Frank Zappa first heard The Persuasions singing over the phone from Krause's store, where Krause managed the group and recorded their first live tracks.
- Krause's acts reached the national stage: The Persuasions charted on Billboard (Street Corner Symphony, No. 16 R&B and No. 88 pop, 1972), and 14 Karat Soul performed on Saturday Night Live in 1983 and drew large audiences in Japan.
- He founded the United in Group Harmony Association (UGHA) and produced live vocal-harmony concerts for decades under the banner Classic Harmony Productions.
- In 2015, Jersey City Mayor Steven Fulop honored him with a proclamation as “Stan the Music Man of Jersey City,” and in 2016 he received the Jersey City Living Legend Award.
- Krause died on May 20, 2016, at the age of 74, and his store closed in 2018.
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Mourning the Passing of Stan Krause, Acappella Icon, Record Store Owner, Producer, Label Owner (2016); Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; Catamount discography — Discogs
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-stan-krause.html

## Stan's Square Records
Record store · Venue · Label home — 1965–2018

- Stan's Square Records was a Jersey City record store founded by Stan Krause in 1965 in Journal Square, later moving to 349 Bergen Avenue
- For five decades the store was a cornerstone for music lovers and record diggers, known especially for preserving and promoting acappella, soul, and rhythm and blues
- The store sold mixtapes to the whole community from the city's top mixtape DJs, including DJ 007, DJ Mad Money, DJ E Double, DJ NEL E NEL, DJ Wimpy B, DJ Big Time, DJ Dyce, DJ Ju-Town, DJ DX, DJ Wizard, DJ Madden, DJ Walley Katt & X5, Semaj da DJ, and many more
- Krause founded Catamount Records (1964), the label that launched the recording careers of vocal groups including The Persuasions and 14 Karat Soul; Frank Zappa first heard The Persuasions singing over the phone from the store, where Krause was the group's manager
- Krause's Catamount acts reached the national stage: The Persuasions charted on Billboard (Street Corner Symphony, No. 16 R&B and No. 88 pop, 1972), and 14 Karat Soul performed on Saturday Night Live (1983) and drew large audiences in Japan
- Krause founded the United in Group Harmony Association (UGHA) and produced music and live vocal-harmony concerts
- In 2015, Jersey City Mayor Steven Fulop issued a proclamation for the store's 50th anniversary honoring Krause as 'Stan the Music Man of Jersey City'; in 2016 he received the Jersey City Living Legend Award
- Krause died on May 20, 2016, at age 74; the store closed in 2018 following his passing
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Mourning the Passing of Stan Krause, Acappella Icon, Record Store Owner, Producer, Label Owner (2016); Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; Stan's Square Records — Facebook page, Jersey City NJ
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-stans-square-records.html

## Strong Vic
DJ · Producer

- Strong Vic is a Jersey City DJ, producer, and beatmaker whose work runs through the heart of the city's mixtape era.
- He is remembered above all for his mixtape intros — the custom drops and original beats that opened other DJs' tapes — a signature that made him one of the scene's most sought-after collaborators and left his fingerprint on a generation of Jersey City mixtapes.
- A talented beatmaker, he built original production alongside his DJ work and is counted among the peers of DJ DX and the Stan's Square Records generation of city DJs.
- He remains active in Jersey City under the handle @strongvic201, the 201 area code marking his place in the Hudson County DJ lineage.
Sources: Instagram @strongvic201 — community identification, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-strong-vic.html

## Suzy Q
Vocalist

- Suzy Q is a Jersey City-based artist who has been recognized as an active participant in the city's music scene.
- Documented during the archive's comprehensive survey, she adds her unique perspective to the local creative landscape.
- Her work is part of the broader independent arts movement thriving within the diverse neighborhoods of Jersey City.
- She engages with her audience through grassroots promotion, embodying the DIY spirit of the region.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-suzy-q.html

## Sweet Slick and Slide
Hip-hop crew — c. 1980 – 1990s

- Sweet Slick and Slide — also styled Sweet Slick and Sly and known as Triple S — was one of Jersey City's dominant hip-hop crews of the 1980s and early 1990s, founded by Lord Sun Albee Al.
- By the accounts of peers, it was one of only two Chilltown crews — with the Tranquilizing Three — considered strong enough to battle the celebrated crews of New York, and it toured widely along the East Coast.
- Its members included Jimmy Mack, Warren "Warm Daddy" Pray, the DJ Victor Quick, and Sugar Ray; founder Lord Sun Albee Al anchored a hip-hop family whose next generations include his children DJ Thunderkatt, the rapper Albee Al, and J-Sass, and his nephew DJ E Double.
- Their hit was It's Magic, named on a period breakin contest flyer that pictures the crew doing it, preserved in this archive.
- A period flyer bills them at The Showdown For All DJs at the Benmore Skating Rink on Communipaw Avenue alongside Strang D, Jimmy M, Al Bee, Capone, Sir Brown, and the era's crews.
- Community tribute from the Stegman Street block, their home ground, credits the crew as the first to bring Doug E. Fresh to Jersey City, at the Armory, when he had only one single out.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube; Family history, The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Period flyers preserved in this archive: the It's Magic breakin contest flyer and The Showdown For All DJs at Benmore Skating Rink (see the images on this page); Young Flizo (@youngflizo), Stegman Street Legacy tribute post, Instagram, January 19, 2022
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-sweet-slick-and-slide.html

## Taylor Portt
Rapper

- Taylor Portt is a creative artist from Jersey City whose work was documented in the archive's survey of the active music scene.
- Her presence highlights the broad range of voices and styles that coexist within the city's artistic community.
- Operating independently, she utilizes contemporary channels to share her music and connect with listeners.
- She represents the grassroots tier of creators who form the vital foundation of the Jersey City Sound.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-taylor-portt.html

## TGK
Hip-hop group — 1995

- TGK were a mid-1990s hip-hop group whose known release, the single and EP “Avenue Ride,” came out in 1995 on Catamount Records — Stan Krause's Jersey City label — in a co-release with Trumpet Records.
- Issued on a 12-inch vinyl and a CD single dated November 4, 1995, the record collected several mixes of the song, among them a Radio Version, a Long Street Version, and the Spider D Club Mix.
- The release places the group in the Jersey City lineage of Catamount and Trumpet, the small local labels through which much of the city's street-level talent was issued; biographical detail on the individual members is scarce and is not printed here pending a primary source.
Sources: TGK — Avenue Ride (12″ vinyl, 1995), Discogs; TGK — Avenue Ride (CD single, 1995), Discogs; Avenue Ride — EP by TGK, Apple Music
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tgk.html

## The A-Team
Rap duo — c. 1999 – 2006

- The A-Team were an early-2000s Jersey City rap duo of Ransom and Hitchcock, celebrated for their punchline-heavy, grimy street lyricism.
- Managed by Orlando Wharton and affiliated with DJ Clue's Desert Storm, they built a strong underground following through their Hardhood Classics mixtape series and ties to artists such as Joe Budden and Stack Bundles.
- After a major-label deal did not materialize the duo split around 2006; Ransom went on to a prolific solo career and Wharton to a landmark executive run.
Sources: Industry and community documentation, The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-a-team.html

## The Black Hollies
Musical group

- The Black Hollies are a rock band from Jersey City, New Jersey, formed in the 2000s and known for their heavy debt to 1960s British Invasion and American garage rock aesthetics.
- They have released albums on Ernest Jenning Record Co. and toured alongside acts including The Raveonettes and The Black Angels, building a following in the rock underground.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Black Hollies; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-black-hollies.html

## The Catamounts
Soul & R&B group — mid-1970s

- The Catamounts were an influential soul and rhythm-and-blues ensemble from Jersey City, emerging during the mid-1970s regional music explosion and becoming a prominent representative of the “Jersey City Sound.”
- Distinct from the strictly instrument-free, street-corner a cappella acts that preceded them, they bridged classic vocal harmony with the orchestration-backed, danceable deep soul that paved the way for early disco.
- They recorded for Catamount Records, the Jersey City label owned by Stan Krause, who also owned the group.
- Decades later the group is featured on “Blessings” by DJ DX, carrying the Catamount sound into a new generation of the city's music.
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; “Blessings” — DJ DX (feat. The Catamounts), YouTube; Catamount discography — Discogs
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-catamounts.html

## The Cobras
Hip-hop group

- The Cobras are a Jersey City-based musical group documented in the archive's survey of the city's diverse arts community.
- Their inclusion in the roster points to the wide variety of ensembles operating within the city's borders across different genres.
- They participate in the local live music ecosystem, contributing to the cultural fabric of their neighborhoods.
- The group stands as an example of the grassroots musical activity that forms the foundation of the Jersey City Sound.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-cobras.html

## The Components
Musical group

- The Components are an American alternative rock band from New Jersey who have built strong connections to the Jersey City independent music scene.
- Drawing on the rich history of Garden State alternative and indie rock, they perform with an energetic, guitar-driven sound.
- They have been active participants in the local live music circuit, playing venues that support original underground and independent artists.
- Their presence underscores Jersey City's role as an incubator for alternative bands looking to establish themselves in the tri-state area.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Components; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-components.html

## The Duprees
Musician

- The Duprees are a Jersey City doo-wop group who had a run of top-ten Billboard hits in the early 1960s, among the most successful vocal groups the city has produced.
- Their highest-charting single, 'You Belong to Me', reached No. 7 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1962, bringing the soft close-harmony doo-wop sound of Jersey City's street corners to a national audience.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Duprees; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-duprees.html

## The Heartaches
Musical group

- The Heartaches were an American doo-wop group from Jersey City, New Jersey, formed in 1962 and active through 1989, one of the city's longest-running vocal groups of the post-Duprees era.
- They recorded and performed through the decline and revival of doo-wop, spanning a twenty-seven-year run that included label recordings and local performance circuit activity.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Heartaches; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-heartaches.html

## The Heat
Hip-hop group

- The Heat is a Jersey City rap group that emerged as part of the city's vibrant and highly competitive hip-hop underground.
- Documented in the archive's survey of active artists, they represent the collective energy of the city's street rap scene.
- Like many local crews, their influence is often felt most intensely at the neighborhood level, circulating through local performances and digital networks.
- They are part of the ongoing continuum of Jersey City hip-hop, carrying forward the collaborative crew dynamic pioneered in earlier eras.
- The lineup photographed on their promo: Murda, Big Panic, and Crisis.
- Their street album HEAT (Tell Em We Coming), hosted by DJ E Double, hit stores June 1, 2002, promoted with a Jersey Journal styled front page preserved in DJ E Double's archive and shown on this page.
Sources: Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; HEAT (Tell Em We Coming) promo artifact, DJ E Double personal archive, 2026 (see image)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-heat.html

## The Jelly Beans
Musical group

- The Jelly Beans were an American rhythm and blues vocal group from Jersey City, New Jersey, United States.
- Formed in 1962 by five high schoolers, the group signed with Jerry Leiber and Mike Stoller's label, Red Bird Records, in 1963.
- Working with songwriters Jeff Barry and Ellie Greenwich, the group released its debut single, "I Wanna Love Him So Bad", in 1964; the song became a hit in the United States, peaking at No. 9 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1964.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Jelly Beans; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-jelly-beans.html

## The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
Documentary film — 2006

- Directed by DJ Wizard (WizTV), the Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 is a nearly three-hour (2:57:58) film documenting Jersey City's mixtape DJ scene as it existed in 2006, the most important single document of that era.
- The film features interviews and home-studio performances from DJ Flash, Mista Quietman, Mark Cee, Styles 007, Man Money, DJ E Double (Double Platinum Entertainment), DJ BigTime, DJ Dolo, DJ DX, X5, Chameleon (Ka-Million), Semaj da DJ, DJ Madden, Benito, Joe Bananas, cover designer Blaze, and others.
- Bonus footage includes the Winterblaze mixtape battle held at the Boys Club (2000), with battle organizer Sean interviewed and DJ Savage among the competitors -- a rare visual record of the city's battle culture.
- The film preserves oral history documenting turntable battles at Audubon Park in the late 1970s and early 1980s where the winner took the loser's equipment, and names scene venues including Rendezvous (formerly Ruthie's), the Boys Club, Lollipop, Taste, and Ferris High School.
- The documentary names and defines Jersey City style markers including blend-driven mixtapes, the 201 style of doubled drums, and stacked-acapella blends, making it the source record for these terms.
Sources: WizTV upload; The Real Mista Quietman upload
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-jersey-city-dj-documentary-2006.html

## The Manhattans
Musical group — 1962 to present

- The Manhattans were an American soul and rhythm and blues vocal group formed in Jersey City, New Jersey, in 1962, and became one of the most successful vocal-harmony groups of the 1970s.
- The original lineup paired lead tenor George “Smitty” Smith with bass singer and principal songwriter Winfred “Blue” Lovett, tenors Edward “Sonny” Bivins and Kenneth “Wally” Kelly, and baritone Richard Taylor.
- Signed to Joe Evans's Carnival Records, the group reached the Billboard R&B chart with “I Wanna Be (Your Everything)” in 1965 and followed it with “Follow Your Heart” and “Baby I Need You,” building a devoted following on the soul and chitlin-circuit stages.
- After the group moved to Deluxe Records, George Smith left in 1970 because of illness and died that December; Gerald Alston stepped in as lead singer, and in 1973 the group signed to Columbia Records.
- On Columbia the Manhattans broke through nationally with “There's No Me Without You” (1973) before their signature hit: “Kiss and Say Goodbye,” written by Winfred Lovett and opened by his spoken monologue, reached No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1976 and was among the first singles ever certified platinum by the RIAA.
- “Shining Star” reached No. 5 on the Hot 100 in 1980 and won the Grammy Award for Best R&B Vocal Performance by a Duo, Group or Chorus.
- Across their career the group charted 45 hits on the Billboard R&B chart, including twelve R&B top-10 singles, their close-harmony ballads carried by Alston's tenor lead and Lovett's bass narrations.
- Richard Taylor left in the mid-1970s after converting to Islam, and Gerald Alston departed for a solo career in 1988, but Bivins and Lovett carried the name onward; the group's records remain staples of quiet-storm and oldies radio and a cornerstone of Jersey City's soul legacy.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Manhattans; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026; Billboard chart history (Hot 100 / Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-manhattans.html

## The Multi-Purpose Solution
Musical group

- The Multi-Purpose Solution is an American rock band hailing from New Jersey with significant ties to the Jersey City music community.
- Their music sits comfortably within the broad spectrum of alternative rock, characterized by dynamic arrangements and intense live performances.
- They have contributed to the regional independent rock ecosystem by consistently performing at local venues and supporting the DIY arts culture.
- The band serves as a testament to the robust, localized rock scenes that thrive across Hudson County and northern New Jersey.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Multi-Purpose Solution; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-multi-purpose-solution.html

## The One & Nines
Musical group

- The One and Nines are an American rhythm and blues band from New Jersey who frequently operate within the Jersey City music scene.
- They draw heavy inspiration from classic 1960s soul and R&B, delivering a vintage sound characterized by tight horn arrangements and powerful vocal performances.
- Their retro-soul aesthetic has made them a popular live act, bringing the energy of classic Stax and Motown records to modern audiences.
- They represent a vital contingent of the New Jersey music community dedicated to preserving and revitalizing traditional R&B.
Sources: Wikipedia — The One & Nines; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-one-and-nines.html

## The Persuasions
A cappella group — 1962 – present

- The Persuasions are an American a cappella group long celebrated as “the kings of a cappella,” whose early career ran through Jersey City
- Stan Krause discovered the group singing on the streets of Jersey City, recorded their first live tracks, and managed them in their early years
- Frank Zappa first heard the group singing over the phone from Stan Krause's Stan's Square Records store; Krause managed the group and issued their early recordings on his Catamount Records label, after which they signed to a major label
- The group charted on Billboard through the early 1970s: Street Corner Symphony (1972) reached No. 16 on the Billboard R&B Albums chart and No. 88 on the Billboard 200; We Came to Play (1971) reached No. 32 R&B; and Spread the Word (1972) reached No. 40 R&B
- The group went on to a decades-long recording career across major and independent labels, influencing generations of a cappella and vocal-harmony singers
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; Billboard chart history (Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Albums and Billboard 200)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-persuasions.html

## The Royal Counts
Vocal group

- The Royal Counts are a vocal-harmony group whose recordings were released on Stan Krause's Jersey City label, Catamount Records
- They are among the doo-wop and a cappella acts Krause and Catamount documented and issued, often in the label's sought-after colored-vinyl pressings
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-royal-counts.html

## The Spellbinders
Soul group — 1965 – 1969

- The Spellbinders were a Jersey City soul and doo-wop group, formed in the mid-1960s by MacArthur Munford with James Wright, Bobby Shivers, Ben Grant, and Elouise Pennington
- Signed to Columbia Records in 1965 under producer Van McCoy, they charted with the Top-25 R&B hit “For You” and “Help Me (Get Myself Back Together Again),” with “Chain Reaction” and “Help Me” hitting in the UK; their album The Magic of the Spellbinders is regarded as a soul classic
- They toured extensively with Jackie Wilson, Joe Tex, and Wilson Pickett before breaking up by 1969
Sources: Obituary of MacArthur Munford, Manifesto magazine (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-spellbinders.html

## The Vice Rags
Musical group

- The Vice Rags are an American rock band from New Jersey with established roots in the Jersey City independent music circuit.
- Their style blends the swagger of classic rock and roll with the raw energy of garage rock and punk.
- They have cultivated a loyal regional following through high-energy live shows and a commitment to authentic, no-frills rock music.
- The band's output is a prime example of the gritty, unpretentious rock tradition long associated with the New Jersey music scene.
Sources: Wikipedia — The Vice Rags; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-vice-rags.html

## The Vocal-Teens
Vocal group · Doo-wop group — 1958 – 1959

- The Vocal-Teens were a Jersey City doo-wop group of the late 1950s, formed while its members were still in high school.
- Their signature side, “Be A Slave,” was written and sung by Michael Dennis Kelly at age fourteen; the group cut it with “Till Then” as demo material in 1958–59, and the two sides sat unreleased until 1972, when Downstairs Records issued them as a 45 (Downstairs 1000).
- The record became a sought-after collector's piece among doo-wop enthusiasts, trading hands at auction decades later and circulating online as an example of uptempo Jersey City doo-wop.
- A group photograph preserved by the late Mike Kelly and surfaced through the doo-wop collector community names the lineup as Mike Kelly (lead on “Be A Slave”), Henry Mays (lead on the flip side), Frederick Worrell, Junior Brooks, and Sonny Campbell.
- The group opened the door for Kelly's professional career: he went on to join fellow Jersey City group The Duprees, becoming a permanent lead singer in 1964.
- Family testimony corroborates the lineup: Errol Mallory — nephew of member Frederick Worrell and named after him — confirmed in a 2026 collector-community thread that “Freddy Errol Worrell” was his father's brother, placing the Worrell family in the heart of Jersey City's 1950s–60s vocal-group scene.
Sources: The Vocal-Teens — Doo-Wop Groups (group history); Vocal-Teens — “Be A Slave” / “Till Then” (Downstairs) — auction record, popsike; “Till Then” 45 label, Downstairs Records 1000 (D-102) — primary source, archived in this entry's gallery; Group photo & lineup — Rare Doo Wop Photos and Footage (community post, from the late Mike Kelly, June 2026)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-the-vocal-teens.html

## Thomas Vincent Cator
Musician

- Thomas Vincent Cator (March 23, 1888 -- April 9, 1931) was an American composer born in Jersey City, New Jersey, whose most significant contribution was the development and advocacy of what he called the aura-modal scale.
- Working in the early twentieth century, Cator published theoretical work on his scale system, an attempt to expand the chromatic possibilities of Western composition beyond standard tonality.
Sources: Wikipedia — Thomas Vincent Cator; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-thomas-vincent-cator.html

## Tia Fuller
Musician

- Tia Fuller (born March 27, 1976) is an American saxophonist, composer, and educator who has been a member of Beyonce's all-female touring band, bringing her to stages in front of millions worldwide.
- She is a faculty member in the ensembles department at Berklee College of Music and has released multiple albums as a leader in the jazz tradition.
- She was a featured jazz musician in Pixar's animated film 'Soul' (2020), bringing her improvisational approach into one of the most widely seen films of that year.
Sources: Wikipedia — Tia Fuller; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tia-fuller.html

## Tom Tallitsch
Musician

- Tom Tallitsch is a jazz saxophonist and composer with ties to Jersey City, New Jersey, who has led his own ensembles and recorded for independent jazz labels.
- He performs primarily on tenor and soprano saxophone and has worked with a range of musicians in the New York metropolitan jazz scene.
Sources: Wikipedia — Tom Tallitsch; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tom-tallitsch.html

## Tommy West
Producer

- Tommy West is an American musician and record producer from Jersey City, New Jersey, best known as the longtime producer and creative partner of Jim Croce. He produced Croce's Billboard Hot 100 number one singles 'Bad, Bad Leroy Brown' (1973) and 'Time in a Bottle' (1973); Croce's 1972 album 'You Don't Mess Around with Jim' topped the Billboard 200, while its title single peaked at No. 8 on the Hot 100.
- He co-ran Terry Cashman and Tommy West Productions, one of the more successful independent production companies of the early 1970s, and continued working in recording and production after Croce's death in 1973.
Sources: Wikipedia — Tommy West; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tommy-west.html

## Tomorrow's Edition
Funk group · Soul group · Vocal group — c. 1981 – 1987

- Tomorrow's Edition were a funk and soul vocal group of the Jersey City scene of the 1980s; the Jersey City label Mel-O Records was founded by producer Mel Odoms specifically to release their records.
- Their run opened with the single "U Turn Me On" (Mel-O MLO-437, 1981, picked up by CBS) and peaked with the album A Song for Everyone (Atlantic 80009-1, 1982), produced by Amir Bayyan of the Kool & the Gang family and remembered by collectors as one of the strongest lesser-known major-label soul albums of its era.
- They continued on Mel-O through the 1980s with "I'm The Best" (1984) and "Million Dollars" (1987), and sang background vocals on Kool & the Gang recordings.
Sources: Mel-o Records, Discogs label page (founded to release Tomorrow's Edition; Jersey City, NJ); Tomorrow's Edition, A Song for Everyone (Atlantic 80009-1, 1982), Soul and Funk Music; Tomorrow's Edition, A Song for Everyone, Discogs master release
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tomorrows-edition.html

## Tony Velona
Musician

- Anthony Velona (November 16, 1920 – January 31, 1986) was an American author, lyricist, and composer.
- Velona was born in Jersey City, New Jersey.
- He wrote or co-wrote numerous songs including the 1955 hit Domani, the 1962 hit Lollipops and Roses, and the 1966 hit Music to Watch Girls By.
Sources: Wikipedia — Tony Velona; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tony-velona.html

## Tranquilizing Three
Hip-hop crew — c. 1980 – 1980s

- The Tranquilizing Three (also Tranquilizer 3) were an early Jersey City hip-hop crew out of the Duncan Projects, formed around 1980 from the earlier group Phase Three.
- Alongside Sweet Slick and Slide, they were regarded as one of the two Chilltown crews good enough to hold their own against New York's crews, and they secured one of the scene's earliest record deals.
- Members associated with the group across its run include Joey Time, Shaz, the DJ Razelle, and Cash Money.
Sources: Chill Town J.C. Hip-Hop Documentary Film, dir. Champagne — YouTube
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tranquilizing-three.html

## Tris McCall
Musician

- Tris McCall is a music journalist, novelist, and rock musician from Hudson County, New Jersey, described by The New York Times as 'the plugged-in, Internet-era muse of Jersey City.'
- He became the music critic for the Newark Star-Ledger in 2010 and has released four solo albums, making him one of the few figures in Jersey City's creative community to work simultaneously as a practicing musician and a respected music critic.
- His McCall's Almanac web project combines short stories with previews of unreleased songs, positioning him at the intersection of literary and musical culture in a way that is specific to Jersey City's arts scene.
Sources: Wikipedia — Tris McCall; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-tris-mccall.html

## Vic Juris
Musician

- Victor Edward Jurusz Jr. (September 26, 1953 -- December 31, 2019), known as Vic Juris, was an American jazz guitarist from Jersey City, New Jersey, widely respected in the New York jazz world for his melodic approach and harmonic sophistication.
- He recorded and performed with Richie Cole, Barry Miles, and other major jazz figures, and released several well-regarded albums as a leader from the 1970s through the 2010s.
Sources: Wikipedia — Vic Juris; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-vic-juris.html

## Vintage
A cappella group

- Vintage is a Jersey City a cappella group formed by MacArthur Munford of the Spellbinders with Ray Block of the Valitons
- The group's recordings were issued through Stan Krause's Catamount Records; the ensemble later continued as New Vintage and remained active in the New York area
Sources: Classic Urban Harmony — Catamount Records label history; Obituary of MacArthur Munford, Manifesto magazine (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-vintage-jersey-city.html

## Wally Brandt
Musician

- Wally Brandt (born Walter Anthony Brandt) is an American guitarist, producer, and songwriter from Jersey City, New Jersey, whose career has spanned pop rock, country music, and production music.
- He is best known for his work with the pop rock band Seven and the Sun, the country music band Whiskey Falls, and as a co-founder of the production music company We3Kings Music.
- His session and production credits place him across multiple genres, and his We3Kings catalog has licensed music for film, television, and advertising.
Sources: Wikipedia — Wally Brandt; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-wally-brandt.html

## Will Oakland
Musician

- Will Oakland (January 15, 1880 – May 15, 1956) was an American countertenor famed for his exceptionally high vocal range.
- He was born Herman Hinrichs in Jersey City, New Jersey, to German-American immigrant parents.
- Oakland began his musical career after leaving the United States Army in 1905, joining Lew Dockstader's minstrels in Rochester, New York.
Sources: Wikipedia — Will Oakland; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-will-oakland.html

## William W. Gilchrist
Musician

- William Wallace Gilchrist (January 8, 1846 -- December 20, 1916) was an American composer born in Jersey City, New Jersey, and a major figure in nineteenth-century Philadelphia's musical life.
- He founded the Mendelssohn Club of Philadelphia in 1874, one of the oldest choral organizations in the United States, and served as its director for decades.
- He received the Cincinnati Festival Prize in 1882 for his Symphony in C major, making him one of the more recognized American orchestral composers of his era.
Sources: Wikipedia — William W. Gilchrist; Archive research: The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-william-w-gilchrist.html

## Wilson El Divino
Recording artist · Songwriter

- Wilson El Divino is a Jersey City recording artist, songwriter, and brand strategist working in the tristate area's Latin urban music scene.
- Of Dominican and Puerto Rican descent, he draws on his Afro-Caribbean background throughout his music and worldview.
- He describes his sound as “Afroton del Norte,” a blend of reggaeton, Afroton, and Afro-Caribbean soul that carries conscious, socially aware lyrics rather than standard party tropes, and releases music under the slogan “MUNDIAL, pero no para todo el MUNDO” (Global, but not for everyone).
- Rooted in the Northeast mixtape circuit, he has collaborated and promoted alongside Jersey City figures including DJ DX and DJ Madden; his tracks include “Gone (Spanglish),” “Diosa,” “Complicada,” and “Futura Ex.”
- He is a cousin of the late artist Liqui Loco, who appeared on regional urban tracks such as the “Dominicans Bang Bang (Remix)” alongside Juelz Santana, DJ DX, and J.R. Writer.
- Outside of music, he is a creative strategist and the founder of the independent cultural brand and platform Cave of the Underworld.
Sources: Artist biography and discography contributed to The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-wilson-el-divino.html

## Wiztv
DJ · Videographer · Documentarian · Blend tape producer · Director · Archivist — 2006–present

- Wiztv — formerly billed as DJ Wizard, born John Freeman — is a Jersey City DJ, videographer, and documentarian who has been archiving the city's music scene on video since at least 2006.
- He directed and produced 'WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1' (2006), a nearly three-hour document of Jersey City's mixtape DJ scene that remains the primary visual record of that era.
- He is the producer of the Jersey Club Blends mixtape series, including Vol. 3 (2017), and created a blend with DJ Sliink titled 'Not Gunna Get Us' that circulated widely on SoundCloud.
- He built the WizTV video platforms — long a leading source for Jersey club party and sweet-sixteen footage across New Jersey — and, to preserve and properly credit the culture, has moved his video archive from YouTube to Patreon.
- As of 2026, he is producing a new WizTV Jersey Club Documentary, with Part 1 previewed via Patreon and Instagram, continuing his role as the scene's primary visual historian.
- Widely recognized as the originator of visual coverage for Jersey club culture, he was the first and most consistent figure to document the scene from the inside — capturing party energy, dancers, DJs, and promoters across New Jersey long before social media or viral dance platforms existed.
- As a videographer and director he is regarded as a pioneer to Newark’s Jersey club DJs, whose parties, sets, and battles he filmed and preserved as the movement lived and breathed on dance floors.
Sources: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006), WizTV upload; Preserved upload via The Real Mista Quietman channel; Jersey Club Blends SoundCloud ("Dj Wizard aka WIZTV"); DJ Sliink & WizTV blend; WizTV Instagram (new documentary preview, 2026); WizTV on Patreon
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-dj-wizard.html

## X5
DJ — c. 2000s

- X5 is a Jersey City DJ who named himself after the BMW X5, a self-description captured in the 2006 WizTV documentary where he is interviewed alongside his partner Wiley Cat (Walley Katt).
- He is part of the HuF fam alongside Monster A and Gooch, a crew affiliation placing him in Jersey City's broader hip-hop social network of the early 2000s.
- He sold mixtapes through Stan's Square Records with his partner Walley Katt, distributing through the store that served as the commercial hub for the city's entire mixtape scene.
Sources: WizTV Presents: The Jersey City DJ Documentary Vol. 1 (2006)
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-x5.html

## Zigimo
Producer · Multi-instrumentalist · Recording artist — 2018 – present

- Zigimo is an American multi-instrumentalist, producer, and recording artist from Jersey City, New Jersey, working in experimental hip-hop, underground rap, and lo-fi instrumental production.
- A graduate of the Jersey City Arts Program at Henry Snyder High School, he trained on tuba and piano — the tuba shaping his low-end and bassline approach, and the piano serving as the harmonic basis of his songwriting.
- His production centers on texture-heavy beats, distortion, and plugg-adjacent lo-fi digital styles, joining conservatory training to internet-native underground forms.
- He maintains a prolific catalog of albums, mixtapes, and singles on platforms including Spotify and Bandcamp, among them the projects imo, DELTA.77, UNRELEASED PROJECTS, and COLLECTION OF INSTRUMENTALS FROM ZIGIMO (2018–2021).
- His singles and EPs include “couldn't refresh,” “EYE FTM,” “network.1,” “formula,” “bandit/mindset!,” “Testing Code Z,” “SIGNAL.1,” and “banjax.”
- He is a son of Jersey City DJ and producer DJ DX (Robert Van Liew), placing him in the next generation of the city's musical lineage.
Sources: Zigimo — releases on Spotify and Bandcamp; Family and biographical detail contributed by Robert Van Liew (DJ DX), The Jersey City Sound, 2026
URL: https://jerseycitysound.com/entry-zigimo.html
